checklist_items.title: Verify that Reward Vault smart contracts enforce access control for incentive management functions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that functions like adding, offering, or managing incentives are restricted to designated Token Managers as per the mitigation strategy. Check smart contract code for proper access control modifiers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for incentive management might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring only designated Token Managers can execute incentive functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code with access control modifiers, audit reports, or verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Validate that Reward Vault access control enforces that only designated Token Managers can add or modify incentives
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Reward Vault smart contract implements strict access controls verifying that only authorized Token Managers can execute incentive management functions, as the security gap assumes flawed access control.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or code review demonstrating proper access control checks are enforced in the Reward Vault contract.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-factor authentication and secure key custody procedures for Guardian multisig accounts responsible for proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all Guardian multisig accounts have robust key management, involving multi-factor authentication and secure storage, to prevent unauthorized proposal cancellations invoked during governance.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig implementation and operational practices might be vulnerable to key compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Robust multisig implementation, secure key management practices for Guardians, monitoring of cancellation events, and potentially a delay/challenge period for cancellations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing multisig signing procedures, key management protocols, and incident response procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signatures on all governance proposals to ensure non-repudiation and verifiability of origin
checklist_items.description: Require that all proposals submitted to the Governance System are cryptographically signed by the submitter's verified on-chain identity, ensuring non-repudiation and preventing attackers from submitting fraudulent proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System might not verify the authenticity of proposals beyond the sender address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed with the submitter's private key, and verify signatures before processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Proposal submission logs showing signature verification results, signatures matching registered on-chain identities.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication for governance proposal submissions from the Berachain Foundation
checklist_items.description: Verify that proposal submissions from the Foundation require multiple proofs of identity or authority to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions, such as multisig mechanisms or cryptographic signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing compliance with authentication protocols for proposal submissions.

checklist_items.title: Require cryptographic signature verification for all proposal data submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that each proposal submitted by the Foundation is signed with a private key and the signature is verified before processing to prevent tampering or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not verify the integrity of all submitted proposal parameters against a signed payload from the Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require the Foundation to sign proposal payloads with their private key and verify signatures during submission.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing signature verification steps and signature validity.

checklist_items.title: Establish and enforce strict access control and key management processes for validator operator keys used in reward configuration
checklist_items.description: Verify that validator keys used to set reward allocations are secured with hardware modules, multi-signatures, or other robust controls to prevent impersonation and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's operational key used for configuring reward allocations is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use Hardware Security Modules (HSMs) for key storage, implement multi-signature approval flows, and enforce key rotation policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access and configuration changes with timestamp and operator identity.

checklist_items.title: Implement code auditing and formal verification of Reward Vault and PoL mechanism smart contracts involved in reward distribution and incentive management
checklist_items.description: Verify that smart contracts managing reward allocations, incentive rate setting, and reward distribution enforce constraints correctly and are free of known vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are specific constraints on reward allocations; an exploitable flaw exists.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct thorough audits, formal verification, and bug bounty programs for related contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification results showing enforcement of constraints.

checklist_items.title: Verify that the Reward Vault smart contract enforces strict access control on incentive management functions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only the authorized Token Manager address can add or modify incentives in the Reward Vault, preventing spoofing or unauthorized modifications. This aligns with mitigation strategy: 'Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring only the designated Token Manager address can execute management functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports confirming access control checks in the smart contract.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature or hardware security module (HSM) protections for Token Manager private keys
checklist_items.description: Secure the private keys used by Token Managers to prevent private key compromise that could lead to spoofing or unauthorized incentive offers, as per mitigation strategy: 'Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The private key of a legitimate Token Manager could be compromised, allowing an attacker to act as them.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multi-signature wallets or HSMs for private key storage and signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports of key management practices and hardware wallet deployment.

checklist_items.title: Require explicit transaction parameter verification in Wallets used by Token Managers
checklist_items.description: Token Managers should meticulously verify all incentive-related transaction parameters before signing, aligning with mitigation strategy: 'Always verify all transaction parameters before signing.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use trusted wallet interfaces with display verification and transaction review features.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction review screens showing verified parameters.

checklist_items.title: Set upper and lower bounds for incentive parameters within smart contracts and enforce them
checklist_items.description: Implement hardcoded or configurable limits on incentive parameters such as rate and deposit amounts to prevent malicious tampering, according to mitigation: 'Establish clear internal guidelines, review processes, and potentially multi-sig controls for setting/changing incentive rates.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective hard-coded upper bounds or sanity checks for incentive rates in the smart contract.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Incorporate on-chain constraints, soft caps, and validation logic to prevent exceeding limits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports confirming enforcement of bounds.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring and alerting for abnormal reward allocation patterns
checklist_items.description: Regularly monitor reward distribution and validator reward allocations for anomalies, aligning with mitigation: 'Implement thorough monitoring of validator performance and network health.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Reward Vault can be whitelisted but later become non-functional or problematic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy monitoring tools to track reward allocations and validator activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs showing active monitoring and alerts for anomalies.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control for incentive management functions within the Reward Vault smart contract.
checklist_items.description: Ensure only designated Token Managers can add, offer, or modify incentives by enacting and enforcing access restrictions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or contract code demonstrating access restrictions.

checklist_items.title: Require on-chain multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions from the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.description: Strengthen the proposal submission process by verifying the submitter's identity and preventing impersonation or spoofed proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures. Regularly audit access and authorization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed payload or blockchain signature verifying proposal origin.

checklist_items.title: Establish cryptographic signing of all proposal data by the Berachain Foundation to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.description: Require that the Foundation's proposal submissions be accompanied by a cryptographic signature that covers all proposal parameters, ensuring integrity and non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The whitelisting proposal data could be altered by an attacker before being finalized by the Governance System if integrity checks are insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require the Foundation to sign the entire proposal payload with its private key, and verify this signature before processing. The Governance System must verify this signature against all parameters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Cryptographic signature verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce rate limiting and CAPTCHA challenges on Reward Vault whitelisting proposal submissions.
checklist_items.description: Mitigate spam and denial-of-service attempts by introducing submission limits and human verification challenges.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective rate-limiting, anti-spam measures, or submission costs for the RFRV channels.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement CAPTCHAs, submission fees, rate limiting, and filtering mechanisms on the proposal submission platform.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System logs showing submission rates and CAPTCHA success rates.

checklist_items.title: Configure and enforce on-chain limits for incentive parameters such as rates and deposit amounts.
checklist_items.description: Prevent malicious or accidental misconfiguration of incentive parameters by setting hard on-chain constraints and validation checks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective hard-coded upper bounds or sanity checks in the smart contract for incentive rates.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain validation that enforces maximum incentive rates, deposit limits, and soft caps. Include warnings or alerts in the UI.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code audit logs showing enforcement of constraints.

checklist_items.title: Audit and implement robust cryptographic key management practices for Validator operational keys.
checklist_items.description: Ensure validator keys used for proposing blocks and signing attestations are securely generated, stored, rotated, and revoked as needed.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use Hardware Security Modules (HSMs), regular key rotation, multi-party signing, and monitoring of validator activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit logs and compliance reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only designated Token Managers can manage incentives
checklist_items.description: Enforce that functions adding, offering, or managing incentives are restricted to verified Token Manager addresses, as the security gap indicates potential unchecked privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing whitelisted Token Managers authorized for each vault.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-signature authorization for setting incentive rates in Reward Vaults to prevent tampering
checklist_items.description: Enforce that any change to incentive parameters, such as rate or deposit amount, requires multisig approval to mitigate the risk of malicious or accidental tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always verify transaction parameters meticulously before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction records showing multisig approval for incentive parameter changes.

checklist_items.title: Establish maximum/minimum incentive rates and deposit limits within the Reward Vault smart contract to prevent economically unsound configurations
checklist_items.description: Embed hard-coded upper bounds or sanity checks in the smart contract for incentive rates and deposit amounts to avoid setting excessively high or low values that could deplete the incentive budget.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective hard-coded upper bounds or sanity checks in the smart contract for the incentive rate.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement optional warnings or soft limits in the UI, and enforce constraints within the smart contract code to prevent invalid configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report confirming on-chain enforcement of incentive rate limits.

checklist_items.title: Implement continuous monitoring and audit of multisig governance actions with detailed signer event logs
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all multisig activities, such as proposal cancellation or privileged actions, are logged with detailed signer information to enable attribution and accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System or the underlying multisig mechanism might not emit detailed, attributable audit logs.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure the multisig contract and governance system to emit detailed events logging all individual signers for critical actions like proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain event logs showing signers for recent governance actions.

checklist_items.title: Adopt secure key management practices and periodic key rotation for validator private keys used in consensus
checklist_items.description: Reduce the risk of key compromise leading to spoofed validator actions by ensuring keys are stored securely, rotated regularly, and managed with hardware security modules (HSMs) or equivalent protections.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement key management best practices including secure storage, multi-factor authorization, and regular rotation of validator keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation and key rotation logs.

checklist_items.title: Deploy anti-phishing measures and verify authenticity of governance proposal submissions from the Foundation
checklist_items.description: Require that submissions to the Governance System (Component 29) originate from a verified, securely managed, and possibly multisignature-controlled Foundation account to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions, and require proposals to be signed by a multisig wallet held by the Foundation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Proposal submission logs with signature verification records.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only legitimate Token Managers can add or modify incentives.
checklist_items.description: The security gap indicates the Reward Vault may not correctly authenticate Token Manager actions. Implement access control checks in the contract to restrict functions to designated Token Manager addresses.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control checks during incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require all incentive management transactions to be verified and signed through secure, trusted interfaces, and verify transaction parameters before signing.
checklist_items.description: The strategy recommends that Token Managers utilize secure interfaces and diligently verify all parameters. This mitigates risks from frontend tampering during incentive parameter updates.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction verification steps, code review reports for UI components.

checklist_items.title: Establish adequate funding and resource monitoring for Reward Vaults to prevent Denial of Service due to resource exhaustion.
checklist_items.description: Mitigation involves ensuring token and gas sufficiency, diligent auditing for bugs, and monitoring limits such as maximum incentive types per vault. This reduces the risk of operational DoS.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults have a functional limit on the number of distinct active incentive types.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must ensure sufficient gas and incentive tokens, Reward Vaults should be audited, and capacity limits monitored and enforced.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Resource utilization logs, capacity monitoring reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement internal guidelines, review processes, and multi-signature controls for setting incentive rates.
checklist_items.description: Establish procedures and controls to prevent accidental or malicious setting of unreasonably high incentive rates, safeguarding financial health.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective hard-coded upper bounds or sanity checks for incentive rate settings in the smart contract.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Protocols should establish guidelines, review processes, and possibly multi-sig controls for setting/changing incentive rates. Implement optional warnings or soft limits in UI. Existing rules (e.g., rates cannot be decreased when active) provide partial mitigation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of governance procedures and records of incentive rate changes.

checklist_items.title: Conduct rigorous security audits and formal verification of the reward allocation smart contracts, including limits and constraints enforcement.
checklist_items.description: Ensures that validators cannot set reward allocations that exceed system-defined constraints, preventing elevation of privileges through improper configuration.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Potential vulnerabilities exist in the smart contracts managing reward allocations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive audits of contracts, formal verification where feasible, and implement on-chain validation checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proofs, transaction logs showing enforcement of constraints.

checklist_items.title: Implement transparency and monitor validator reward allocations and emission settings.
checklist_items.description: Promotes openness and accountability, deterring malicious behavior and enabling community oversight.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validators might be incentivized to direct $BGT emissions to vaults offering off-chain benefits.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure detailed, accessible on-chain records of reward allocations and emissions; promote transparency in validator behaviors.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain data analyses, community reports on validator reward distributions.

checklist_items.title: Verify that Reward Vault contracts enforce caller authentication for incentive management functions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only the designated Token Manager addresses can execute functions to add, offer, or manage incentives within Reward Vaults based on access control logic.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring only authorized Token Managers can execute incentive-related functions. These controls must be coded explicitly, and the contract should be reviewed for correctness.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control enforcement during incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require secure transaction verification for incentive parameter changes
checklist_items.description: Advise Token Managers to verify all incentive setting parameters (rate, amount, token addresses) before signing and submitting transactions to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use trusted interfaces, verify transaction details meticulously before signing, and dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI). Encourage direct interactions via trusted wallets. Implement UI warnings and multi-factor checks if possible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction confirmation dialogs showing verified parameters.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain limits or sanity checks on incentive rate and parameters
checklist_items.description: Enhance smart contract logic to include maximum thresholds and sanity checks on incentive rates and deposit amounts, especially for 'set rate' functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Protocols should establish internal guidelines, review processes, and potentially multi-sig controls for setting/changing incentive rates.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Incorporate hardcoded upper bounds and validation logic within smart contracts for incentive parameters. Use multi-signature approvals where appropriate.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test results or audit reports demonstrating validation logic enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Ensure multisig `Cancel` operations require all signers to be verified and logged
checklist_items.description: Configure multisig contract to require all signatures to be from verified Guardians, with individual signers' identities logged in immutable events.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or the Governance System might not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multisig implementations that emit detailed events including each signer. Maintain on-chain and off-chain logs linking signers to actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Event logs showing individual signatures and signers for each cancellation.

checklist_items.title: Secure Guardian key management and implement threshold multisig safeguards
checklist_items.description: Utilize hardware security modules (HSMs), enforce multi-factor authentication, and regular key rotation for Guardian keys, with audits of multisig configuration.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig implementation or the management processes might have vulnerabilities or be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy hardware-backed key storage, perform regular key rotation, and conduct security audits of multisig setup.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, operational procedures, and key management logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication and signed proposals for Reward Vault whitelisting submissions
checklist_items.description: Require that all Reward Vault whitelisting proposals submitted by the Berachain Foundation are signed with a cryptographic key, and verified via signature validation on-chain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be accompanied by a cryptographic signature from a pre-registered, verified key or multisig address. The Governance System must validate these signatures against known public keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain verification logs confirming signature validation during proposal processing.

checklist_items.title: Enforce end-to-end integrity verification for proposal data submissions
checklist_items.description: Utilize on-chain digital signatures to verify that all data in Reward Vault proposals submitted by the Foundation remain unaltered from the original signed payload.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Proposal data submitted by the Foundation could be altered in transit or during storage if integrity is not verified.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require signed proposal payloads, and the Governance System must verify signatures and compare payload hashes before processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs showing signature verification and payload hash matching.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate-limiting, CAPTCHAs, and spam filters for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Set limits on the number of submissions from a single source, include CAPTCHA challenges, and employ spam filtering to prevent flood attacks on the proposal review system.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV review process may not have robust mechanisms to verify the true identity and legitimacy of every submitting project/developer, making it vulnerable to spam floods.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy rate limits, CAPTCHA challenges, and automated spam detection systems on the proposal submission platform.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System logs demonstrating rate-limiting in effect, CAPTCHA completion, and spam filter logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify that Reward Vault contracts enforce access control to restrict incentive management functions to designated Token Managers.
checklist_items.description: Ensure Reward Vault smart contracts implement access control mechanisms that allow only the designated Token Manager addresses to add, offer, or manage incentives, as per mitigation strategy from threat 1.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code review or audit logs confirming access restrictions are properly implemented.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-signature approval for setting incentive rates in Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.description: Implement a multi-sig or multi-party approval process for changes to incentive rates or deposit parameters, especially as per mitigation from threat 2, to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of incentive rate change transactions, multisig approval records.

checklist_items.title: Implement rigorous validation for reward allocation percentages in PoL mechanisms.
checklist_items.description: Ensure on-chain validation logic prevents total reward allocations from exceeding 100% or individual vault caps, to mitigate tampering risk as per threat 50.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are specific constraints on reward allocations (e.g., total must not exceed 100%, individual vaults may have caps like 30%).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure rigorous testing and auditing of smart contracts managing reward allocations to verify that all constraints are correctly and robustly enforced. Implement on-chain checks that prevent invalid allocations from being committed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Unit test results, audit reports verifying constraints are enforced in the code.

checklist_items.title: Review and verify the security of off-chain proposal submission channels for Signatures and Data Integrity.
checklist_items.description: Implement end-to-end security measures for proposal/submission platforms (e.g., HTTPS, signatures) to mitigate tampering risks as highlighted in threat 22.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The whitelisting proposal data submitted by the Berachain Foundation (Actor 8) could be altered by an attacker before being finalized.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure, access-controlled channels for submitting RFRVs containing sensitive information. Provide clear guidelines on what information will be public vs. private. Allow for phased disclosure if necessary.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of submission timestamps, signatures, or security protocols in place.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all proposals, including RFRVs, are signed with cryptographically verifiable signatures linked to the submitter.
checklist_items.description: Require that all governance proposals and RFRV submissions be signed with the submitter's private key, verified during on-chain processing, to prevent spoofing or tampering, as per threat 21.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance system (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures. Regularly audit access and authorization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain evidence of signatures matching known Foundation addresses; audit logs verifying signature verification during proposal processing.

checklist_items.title: Establish and enforce strict proposal creation policies, including proposal fee requirements and rate limits.
checklist_items.description: Implement mechanisms to prevent spam and flooding of governance proposals, as recommended in mitigation from threat 42, through proposal deposit requirements and rate limiting.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance system (Component 29) might be overwhelmed by spam proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement an appropriate $BGT deposit requirement for creating proposals, which can be slashed for malicious/spam proposals. Ensure robust UI filtering in BeraHub. Active monitoring by community and Guardians.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing proposal deposits, rate limiting mechanisms, or filters in place.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure key management and operational procedures for Guardians involved in multisig actions.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate risks related to key compromise (threat 11), enforce multi-factor authentication, periodic key rotation, and secure storage for Guardians' keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or the Governance System does not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits of the Guardian multisig contract. Secure, transparent, and verifiable processes for Guardian election, rotation, and revocation. Strong key management and operational security for all legitimate Guardians.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, key management policies, key rotation records.

checklist_items.title: Develop and implement incident response procedures for multisig key compromise.
checklist_items.description: Prepare protocols for swift action if Guardian keys are suspected to be compromised, including key revocation, governance delays, or alternative governance mechanisms to mitigate threats like 11.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig contract or the Guardian election process could be compromised, resulting in unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish procedures for rapid key revocation, multi-party confirmation requirements, and fallback governance mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of incident response plans, test drills, or revocation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only designated Token Managers can modify incentives
checklist_items.description: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding or managing incentives might be flawed, allowing unauthorized modifications if not properly secured. Implement and enforce role-based access restrictions and consider multi-signature approval for sensitive functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control restrictions and role assignments.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure and verified transaction interfaces for the Token Manager to prevent tampering with incentive parameters
checklist_items.description: Token Managers may use compromised interfaces or malicious frontends to alter incentive parameters before submission. Use secure, vetted wallets and interfaces, and verify all transaction details meticulously before signing.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software.

checklist_items.title: Configure reward allocation settings with on-chain constraints and validations to prevent malicious or erroneous modifications
checklist_items.description: Smart contracts managing reward allocations should enforce immutable or verified constraints (e.g., total percentage limits). Implement on-chain validation logic and review processes to prevent setting invalid allocations.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure rigorous testing and auditing of the smart contracts managing reward allocations to verify that all constraints are correctly and robustly enforced.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Contract audit reports and test case results verifying constraint enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all proposals submitted by the Foundation to ensure data integrity and authenticity
checklist_items.description: Proposal data from the Foundation should be signed with a private key, and verification logic should be enforced on-chain to prevent tampering or impersonation.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain proof of proposal signature matching the Foundation's approved public key.

checklist_items.title: Enhance multisig security and operational procedures for Guardian keys, including key management, operational security, and event logging
checklist_items.description: Guardians' multisig keys should be stored securely using hardware security modules, with clear operational procedures, regular key rotation, and detailed event logging to prevent unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract itself, which authorizes Guardian actions, might have a vulnerability allowing unauthorized execution of the cancel function by a spoofer meeting the threshold with compromised keys.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Robust multisig implementation, secure key management practices, monitoring of cancellation events, and potentially a delay/challenge period for cancellations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of multisig key management and logs of Guardian actions.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels and signatures for proposal cancellation signals to prevent tampering during transmission
checklist_items.description: Cancellation signals should be submitted directly on-chain with cryptographic signatures. If transmitted off-chain, use secure, encrypted channels with verification to prevent data tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: If the cancellation signal (Data Flow 62) is relayed through an insecure off-chain mechanism before reaching the Governance System, it could be altered to target a different proposal or invalidate the signal.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure cancellation signals are submitted directly on-chain by Guardians or via secure, integrity-protected channels. All parameters should be part of the signed transaction.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction logs with signature verification.

checklist_items.title: Implement detailed on-chain event logging for Guardian actions to enhance accountability and prevent repudiation
checklist_items.description: All multisig proposal cancellations should emit events logging individual signers and timestamps, ensuring that Guardian actions are attributable and auditable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or the Governance System does not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure the multisig and Governance System emit detailed events logging all individual signers for critical actions like proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs showing signer endorsements for cancellations.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication practices among Guardians and operational security protocols to prevent premature disclosure of sensitive governance information
checklist_items.description: Guardians should use encrypted communication channels, follow operational security best practices, and avoid publicly disclosing sensitive proposal or cancellation information before on-chain actions are taken.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish secure communication protocols and operational security guidelines for Guardians.

checklist_items.title: Implement operational and technical safeguards to prevent Guardian key compromise, including hardware security modules, key rotation, and multi-factor authentication

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits of the Guardian multisig contract. Secure, transparent, and verifiable processes for Guardian election, rotation, and revocation. Strong key management and operational security for all legitimate Guardians.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audits and security best practice documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement access control in the Reward Vault smart contract to restrict incentive management functions to designated Token Managers
checklist_items.description: Ensure only authorized Token Managers can add or modify incentives in the Reward Vault, as the security gap indicates possible flawed access control allowing spoofing.

Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control checks during incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require all incentive parameter setting transactions to be verified carefully in the user's wallet before signing
checklist_items.description: Given the risk of tampering with incentive parameters (rate, amount), ensure users review transaction details meticulously before signing, especially when interacting via potentially compromised interfaces.

checklist_items.description: To mitigate the risk of setting unsustainable incentive rates (behavior ID 102), smart contracts should include parameters and enforce rules preventing rates beyond predefined sane thresholds.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Robust multisig implementation, secure key management practices for Guardians, monitoring of cancellation events, and potentially a delay/challenge period for cancellations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit of multisig setup, key management procedures, and recent Guardian activity logs.

checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering in Proposal submissions (behaviors ID 110, 111), mandate that all proposals are accompanied by a cryptographic signature from a verified key of the Foundation, and verify signatures before processing.
