checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control may be flawed, as indicated by the security gap.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring only designated Token Managers can execute incentive management functions. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control enforcement and permission checks.

checklist_items.title: Verify transaction parameters before signing to prevent tampering via malicious frontend or wallet compromise
checklist_items.description: Token Managers and users must diligently verify incentive parameters (e.g., rate, token addresses) in their wallet interfaces before signing transactions, mitigating risks of tampering during interactions with Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised interface or does not verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and trusted wallet interfaces, always verify transaction details meticulously before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction verification in the trusted wallet interface.

checklist_items.title: Ensure only trusted and verified entities submit governance proposals and whitelist requests
checklist_items.description: Implement verification mechanisms such as cryptographic signatures or verified on-chain identities for entities submitting proposals to prevent spoofing or impersonation of the Berachain Foundation or other entities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System might not adequately verify that proposals originate from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed by the Foundation's private keys and verify these signatures before processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs and proposal submission records.

checklist_items.title: Implement end-to-end integrity verification for governance proposals and RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographic signatures from the submitting entity to ensure that proposal and RFRV data is not tampered with during transit or storage, enabling detection and rejection of altered submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal data is vulnerable to modification after being sent but before being recorded by the Governance System.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require signed proposals with cryptographic signatures verified before acceptance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Digital signature verification records and signed proposal files.

checklist_items.title: Establish robust role and key management procedures for Guardians to prevent unauthorized proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Implement strict operational procedures, secure key storage, multi-factor authentication, and regular key rotation for Guardians to mitigate risks of key compromise or misuse leading to spoofed proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or Guardian management processes might have vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt secure key management practices, conduct security audits, and enforce operational security for Guardians.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of Guardian key access and rotation records.

checklist_items.title: Monitor for and prevent Guardian key compromise or unauthorized access through security audits and operational controls
checklist_items.description: Regularly audit Guardian multisig contracts and operational procedures, implement multi-factor authentication, and monitor for suspicious activity to prevent elevation of privilege attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or the Guardian election process might have vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct regular security audits, enforce operational security best practices, and implement anomaly detection for Guardian activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and activity logs showing Guardian actions and key management activities.

checklist_items.title: Secure off-chain communication channels for Guardian actions to prevent tampering or interception
checklist_items.description: Use encrypted, signed, and authenticated communication channels for off-chain Guardian communications (e.g., proposal cancellations) to maintain integrity and prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The cancellation signal is relayed through an off-chain mechanism lacking sufficient integrity protection.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement end-to-end encrypted, signed messaging protocols for off-chain Guardian communications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication logs with cryptographic proofs of integrity.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive on-chain event logging for Guardian actions to facilitate accountability
checklist_items.description: Ensure that multisig and governance contracts emit detailed, immutable events recording individual Guardian signatures and actions for proposal cancellations and other critical operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system might not emit detailed audit logs for multisig Guardian actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Upgrade or configure contracts to produce detailed event logs of Guardian signers and actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs demonstrating individual Guardian signatures for each cancellation.

checklist_items.title: Educate and inform users about verifying transaction details on trusted wallets to prevent tampering and phishing attacks
checklist_items.description: Provide guidelines, warnings, and educational material to users to verify all transaction details (e.g., amounts, recipient addresses) before signing, reducing risks from compromised frontends or malicious sites.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not meticulously verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Develop educational resources and UI features that display transaction details clearly and securely.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User feedback collected during testing and audits of wallet interfaces.

checklist_items.title: Implement security measures on BeraHub frontend to prevent tampering and ensure secure transaction signing
checklist_items.description: Use Content Security Policy (CSP), Subresource Integrity (SRI), HTTPS, and other security best practices to protect BeraHub from tampering that could lead to transaction manipulation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: dApp frontends can be compromised or malicious.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure security headers, audit dependencies, and monitor for malicious content.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports, browser security reports.

checklist_items.title: Use secure, verified, and trusted wallet software that displays transaction details and warns before signing
checklist_items.description: Encourage users to use hardware wallets or trusted wallet providers that clearly display transaction details and warnings to prevent signing tampered transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not verify transaction details properly.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Advocate for the use of hardware wallets and trusted wallet software with user prompts and verification features.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User case reports, wallet software documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification for Reward Vault whitelisting proposal submissions
checklist_items.description: Require that all Reward Vault whitelisting proposals submitted by the Berachain Foundation are signed with a cryptographically verifiable on-chain identity to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not verify the integrity of proposals from the Foundation, allowing spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signatures and on-chain verification of proposal submissions from verified Foundation addresses.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal payloads with on-chain verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access control within Reward Vault smart contracts to restrict incentive management functions to designated Token Managers
checklist_items.description: Implement explicit access control checks in Reward Vault smart contracts so that only authorized Token Managers can add, modify, or remove incentives, preventing spoofing or unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate role-based access control (RBAC) and verify caller addresses within smart contract functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code review and access control audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure key management and operational procedures for Validator private keys
checklist_items.description: Adopt hardware security modules (HSMs), multi-signature processes, and regular key rotation policies to protect Validator operators' keys from compromise, mitigating spoofing risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure key storage (HSMs), multi-sig authorization, and key rotation protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational procedures documentation, key management audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust monitoring and slashing mechanisms for Validator liveness and misbehavior
checklist_items.description: Deploy real-time monitoring systems and enforce slashing penalties for Validator inactivity, malicious behavior, or failure to propose blocks as required, to prevent denial of service attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Failure of validators (especially if widespread or coordinated) can lead to chain halts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Set up performance monitoring, abnormal activity alerts, and enforce slashing rules for non-participation or malicious actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring logs, slashing transaction records.

checklist_items.title: Promote user education and enforce secure data handling for private keys and seed phrases
checklist_items.description: Educate users to store their private keys offline in hardware wallets or secure environments, and avoid malware or phishing threats that could lead to private key disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User's private keys/seed phrase (Asset 18), if disclosed, grant full control over the user's account and assets.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Provide user guides, secure key storage best practices, and promote hardware wallet usage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User education materials, adoption reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction verification and user confirmation steps in user wallets
checklist_items.description: Encourage users to verify all transaction parameters displayed in their wallet interface before signing, especially for token swaps, incentives, and redemptions, to prevent tampering by malicious frontends or malware.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: dApp frontends can be compromised, leading to tampered transaction parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets with transaction review features; display detailed transaction info before signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User wallet transaction logs, UI/UX review reports.

checklist_items.title: Apply HTTPS and content security policies to BeraHub frontend
checklist_items.description: Ensure BeraHub uses HTTPS for all communications and implements Content Security Policy (CSP) and Subresource Integrity (SRI) to prevent injection or tampering of its web interface, reducing risks of tampering and data leakage.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: BeraHub frontend could be a target for phishing or supply chain attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS, implement CSP, SRI, and regularly update dependencies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports, browser security headers verification.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and spam filtering for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Configure submission portals to require CAPTCHAs, impose rate limits, and validate proposal quality, reducing spam and preventing DoS attacks against the review process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV review capacity can be exhausted by fake or low-quality submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add CAPTCHA, enforce rate limits, and set minimum proposal quality standards.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Server logs, anti-spam system reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust access control for Reward Vault management functions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized and authenticated Token Managers can add, modify, or remove incentives within the Reward Vault contract.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating access control enforcement on incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Verify transaction parameters before signing and submission
checklist_items.description: Token Managers should meticulously review all incentive management transaction details (e.g., rate, token address, deposit amount) in a trusted wallet interface prior to signing to prevent tampering or malicious alterations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots or logs showing verification of transaction parameters prior to signing.

checklist_items.title: Configure and monitor reward emission parameters securely with validation checks
checklist_items.description: Ensure that reward allocation settings (e.g., percentages, vault limits) are set within defined constraints, and implement on-chain validations to prevent misconfiguration or malicious inputs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are specific constraints on reward allocations (e.g., total must not exceed 100%, individual vaults may have caps like 30%).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure rigorous testing and auditing of the smart contracts managing reward allocations to verify that all constraints are correctly and robustly enforced.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports or formal verification results confirming enforcement of allocation constraints.

checklist_items.title: Secure key management and multi-factor authentication for validator operations
checklist_items.description: Implement strong operational security practices for validator keys, including hardware security modules, key rotation, and multi-signature approvals for reward configuration and block proposal responsibilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management practices, enforce key rotation, and use multi-signature actions for critical validator operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documents and audit logs of key rotations and multi-signature approvals.

checklist_items.title: Deploy and maintain comprehensive monitoring and slashing mechanisms for validator liveness
checklist_items.description: Ensure validator activity is continuously monitored, and slashing conditions are clear and enforced for non-participation, malicious activity, or misbehavior impacting network availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Failure of validators (especially if widespread or coordinated) can lead to chain halts, significantly increased block times, or failure to reach finality.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement slashing penalties, monitor validator participation, and design robust leader election and fallback mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validator activity logs, slashing event records, and network performance metrics.

checklist_items.title: Implement formal verification and security audits for smart contracts managing reward allocations and PoL mechanics
checklist_items.description: Conduct thorough audits and formal verification of all related smart contracts (e.g., reward distribution, staking, boost) to ensure they enforce constraints and are resilient against exploits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in how validator stake, $BGT boost, or other PoL parameters are calculated or applied could allow a validator to illegitimately increase their influence or rewards.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Carry out independent audits, formal verification, and implement on-chain validation checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit certificates, formal verification reports, and security review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control within Reward Vault smart contracts to ensure only designated Token Manager addresses can modify incentives.
checklist_items.description: The Reward Vault contract's current access control might be flawed, allowing malicious actors to spoof the Token Manager identity and manipulate incentives. Implementing and enforcing strict access controls ensures only authorized addresses can perform incentive management functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing restriction of incentive management functions to designated Token Manager addresses.

checklist_items.title: Require all governance proposals, including Reward Vault whitelisting, to be signed with verifiable cryptographic signatures from authorized representatives of the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.description: To prevent impersonation or spoofing of Foundation proposals, the system should cryptographically verify the origin and integrity of proposal submissions. Requiring signatures from verified Foundation multisig addresses ensures proposals are authentic.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) authenticates proposals from the Berachain Foundation based on a mechanism (e.g., signature from a specific EOA or multisig).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures. Regularly audit access and authorization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of proposal submissions with cryptographic signature verification results.

checklist_items.title: Ensure on-chain proposal submission includes cryptographic signatures from the Foundation's verified multisig wallet and verify these signatures before processing proposals.
checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering or alteration of proposal data, the on-chain governance system must verify that submission data is signed appropriately and matches the on-chain signer address. This prevents off-chain tampering leading to malicious proposals or modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal data is vulnerable to modification after being sent by the Berachain Foundation but before being immutably recorded by the Governance System.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure end-to-end integrity protection for all proposal data by signing payloads before submission and verifying signatures on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Proof of signature verification steps and logs of proposal submission process.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring and alerting for Guardian multisig actions, including proposal cancellations, to promptly detect suspicious activity.
checklist_items.description: Continuous monitoring of multisig transaction events allows early detection of unauthorized or suspicious proposal cancellations. Alerts enable rapid response and potential rollback or investigation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or the Governance System does not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure comprehensive event logging for multisig actions, including each signer's address, and set up alerts for anomalous activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Event logs showing multisig signers and alert logs for suspicious activity.

checklist_items.title: Configure guardian key management with hardware security modules (HSMs) and enforce key rotation policies to prevent unauthorized control over multisig guardians.
checklist_items.description: Secure key storage and regular key rotation mitigate the risk of actor impersonation or key compromise within the Guardian multisig setup. Proper key management practices are essential for maintaining trust and security.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig contract (implied, as Guardians operate via multisig) has a vulnerability in its member management functions (add/remove signers) or its core signature validation logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rigorous key management practices, including hardware security modules (HSMs), multi-factor authentication for management actions, and scheduled key rotation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, hardware key audit logs, and rotation schedules.

checklist_items.title: Ensure that the multisig contract used by Guardians adheres to best security practices, including formal verification and comprehensive security audits.
checklist_items.description: A secure multisig contract reduces the risk of vulnerabilities that could allow unauthorized control or malicious actions. Formal verification and regular audits help identify and fix potential flaws before deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig contract (implied, as Guardians operate via multisig) has a vulnerability in its member management functions or core signature validation logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of the multisig contract, followed by code reviews and community bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification documentation, and bug bounty reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure off-chain communication protocols for Guardians, including end-to-end encryption and multi-factor authentication, to prevent tampering with cancellation signals.
checklist_items.description: Secure communication channels between Guardians and the governance system are critical to prevent man-in-the-middle tampering of cancellation signals, especially if relayed off-chain. E2E encryption protects data integrity and confidentiality.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The cancellation signal (Data Flow 62) is relayed through an intermediary off-chain system before reaching the Governance System.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement end-to-end encryption, digital signature verification, and secure communication protocols for all off-chain Guardian messages.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication channel security architecture, encryption certificates, and logs of message exchanges.

checklist_items.title: Deploy and promote the use of hardware wallets for Users when signing sensitive transactions, such as private key management or crucial operations.
checklist_items.description: Hardware wallets provide a secure environment for private keys, reducing the risk of private key compromise and subsequent repudiation or unauthorized transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User's private keys/seed phrase (Asset 18), if disclosed, grant full control over the user's account and assets.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage users to use hardware wallets, provide educational resources on private key security, and integrate hardware wallet support into wallet interfaces.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User surveys, wallet integration reports, and security best practice documentation.

checklist_items.title: Verify that transaction parameters displayed to users originate from verified sources and are presented with clear, unalterable displays in wallet interfaces.
checklist_items.description: Users should verify transaction details before signing. Wallet interfaces must ensure that the information shown is authentic and has not been tampered with, especially for high-value operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: dApp frontends can be compromised (e.g., DNS hijacking, malicious code injection).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP), Subresource Integrity (SRI), and secure UI practices in frontend development; ensure wallets display transaction details with cryptographic assurance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports, browser security audits, and user testing results.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHA, and submission fees on the RFRV platform to prevent spam or denial-of-service attacks against proposal submissions.
checklist_items.description: To protect the integrity and availability of the proposal review process, submissions should be throttled and authenticated with anti-spam mechanisms, ensuring only genuine proposals are processed.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective rate-limiting, anti-spam measures, or submission costs for the RFRV channels.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement CAPTCHA, submission fees, and rate limiting inside the proposal submission platform.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Submission logs, security controls documentation, and review of platform configurations.

checklist_items.title: Implement and verify strong access controls in the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only legitimate Token Managers can add or manage incentives.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only designated Token Manager addresses with proper authorization can execute incentive management functions. This mitigates spoofing risks by compromised or malicious actors.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing only authorized Token Manager addresses executing incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require direct on-chain submission of governance proposals from the Berachain Foundation with cryptographic signatures to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.description: Mandate that all proposals submitted by the Foundation are signed with a private key verified on-chain, ensuring authenticity and preventing impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) authenticates proposals from the Berachain Foundation based on a mechanism (e.g., signature from a specific EOA or multisig).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require that all proposals are signed with the Foundation's private key and verify signatures on-chain before accepting proposals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain proposal submissions showing cryptographic signatures matching the Foundation's verified public key.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels and cryptographic signing for off-chain proposal data submissions to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.description: Use end-to-end encryption and digital signatures for proposal data transmitted from actors (e.g., Actors 5 and 8) to ensure integrity and prevent tampering during transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The whitelisting proposal data submitted by the Berachain Foundation (Actor 8) could be altered by an attacker before being finalized by the Governance System.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement digital signatures for proposal data; use secure submission portals with TLS and integrity checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal data verified on-chain or in secure submission logs.

checklist_items.title: Educate users to verify transaction details meticulously before signing, especially for transactions involving incentives or asset transfers.
checklist_items.description: Mitigate front-end tampering risks by ensuring users cross-check transaction parameters in their wallets before signing transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: dApp frontends can be compromised (e.g., XSS, malicious code injection) or are intentionally malicious.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong frontend security practices, user education, and clear transaction review procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User acknowledgment logs, security training records.

checklist_items.title: Configure validators with secure key management, rotate keys regularly, and implement network monitoring to prevent Spoofing and Tampering attacks.
checklist_items.description: Ensure validator private keys are stored securely in HSMs, enforce key rotation policies, and monitor validator activity for anomalies to mitigate Spoofing risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules for key storage, enforce multi-factor access controls, and implement activity monitoring with alerting.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management logs, activity reports, incident response documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement validator performance and liveness monitoring with slashing penalties for non-participation or malicious behavior.
checklist_items.description: Regularly monitor validator activity and impose penalties (slashing) for validators that fail to propose blocks or behave maliciously, ensuring network availability and integrity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Failure of validators (especially if widespread or coordinated) can lead to chain halts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy node performance monitoring systems, define slashing conditions, and establish consensus rules for penalties.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validator activity logs, network health dashboards.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all validator consensus messages are signed with cryptographically secure keys and monitor for anomalies to prevent Spoofing and Tampering.
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographic signatures for validator messages and analyze for inconsistent or anomalous activity to detect Spoofing or Tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-factor key management, regular key rotation, and anomaly detection systems.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs, anomaly detection alerts.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access control in Reward Vault smart contracts to ensure only designated Token Managers can add or modify incentives
checklist_items.description: Implement and test smart contract access control mechanisms that restrict incentive management functions to approved Token Manager addresses, as per the mitigation strategy for spoofing threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing that only authorized Token Manager addresses invoke incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require off-chain proposal submissions to be signed with the submitting entity's private key and verify signatures before recording to prevent tampering in Reward Vault whitelisting process
checklist_items.description: Implement cryptographic signature verification on all on-chain proposal submissions from the Berachain Foundation to ensure data integrity and authenticity, directly addressing threat of tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not verify the authenticity of proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require the Berachain Foundation to sign the proposal payload with their private key, and verify this signature on-chain before accepting the proposal. Use secure signature schemes and maintain a public key blacklist/whitelist.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction demonstrating that only proposals with valid signatures from the authorized Foundation address are accepted.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain checks and validation for reward allocation parameters to prevent exceeding maximum permitted percentages or caps
checklist_items.description: Add validation logic to reward distribution mechanisms to enforce constraints such as total allocation percentages not exceeding 100% and individual vault caps, mitigating risks of malicious or erroneous setting of reward allocations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in reward allocation logic could allow a validator to bypass constraints.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce robust on-chain validation of all reward allocation inputs to prevent violations of system constraints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing validation failures when attempts to set invalid allocations are rejected.

checklist_items.title: Require multisignature approval for critical governance proposals and reward configuration changes to prevent elevation of privilege
checklist_items.description: Implement multisig or multi-party approval mechanisms for proposals that alter the Reward Vault whitelists, reward allocations, or other critical governance parameters, reducing the risk of malicious or unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System might not verify that only authorized multisig signers can approve proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposal approval via a multisig wallet with a threshold of trusted members, and verify signatures on-chain before executing proposals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction logs showing approval signatures from a quorum of trusted signers.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure, verified identity onboarding and key management procedures for Guardians participating in multisig governance to prevent unauthorized control
checklist_items.description: Implement rigorous operational security procedures and cryptographic safeguards, including hardware security modules and secure key storage, for Guardians to prevent infiltration, key compromise, and unauthorized influence over governance actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak key management practices could lead to Guardian key compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt best practices for key management, including hardware wallets, multi-factor authentication, regular key rotation, and secure key storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit records of Guardian key management procedures and hardware security module integration.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure, end-to-end integrity verification for off-chain proposal and reward submission data
checklist_items.description: Utilize digital signatures and secure transmission channels (e.g., HTTPS, TLS) for all proposal and reward whitelisting data exchanged between developers and the governance platform to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV submission platform may not have sufficient integrity protections.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require all submissions to be signed with a verifiable digital signature; use secure TLS channels for data transmission; implement cryptographic hashes or checksums.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed submission logs and network transmission records demonstrating use of cryptographic A signatures and secure channels.

checklist_items.title: Maintain detailed, immutable on-chain logs of guardian multisig actions, including signer identities, timestamps, and decision rationale
checklist_items.description: Configure multisig contracts to emit comprehensive events capturing individual signatures, decision timestamps, and roles for all guardian actions such as proposal cancellations, to support accountability and mitigate repudiation risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System or multisig contract may not log detailed signatory data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure multisig contracts to emit events for each signature, including signer address, timestamp, and action details; periodically audit logs for anomalies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs verifying signer identities and timestamps for each guardian action.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit smart contracts related to incentive management, reward distribution, and governance proposals, focusing on access control, logic correctness, and vulnerability mitigation
checklist_items.description: Perform comprehensive security audits and formal verification on smart contracts managing incentives, reward allocations, and governance proposals to detect and correct security flaws that could be exploited for tampering or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may contain vulnerabilities despite audits.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage independent, reputable security auditors; conduct formal verification and bug bounty programs; enforce recommended remediation before deployment.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports with identified vulnerabilities, mitigation measures, and verification results.

checklist_items.title: Configure reward allocation mechanisms to include rate limits and change frequency constraints, with on-chain validation, to prevent unauthorized or malicious control over reward distribution
checklist_items.description: Implement controls within smart contracts that set maximum permissible change frequency or restrict total reward rate adjustments, directly addressing threats related to tampering and privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contract logic does not enforce reward setting constraints.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add on-chain validation functions that check for acceptable ranges and change limits on reward parameters; monitor and alert on unusual activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing validation and enforcement of reward configuration constraints.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures and regular key rotation policies for validator keys to prevent unauthorized signing and spoofing in consensus processes
checklist_items.description: Define secure key lifecycle management policies, including hardware security modules, regular rotation, and multi-factor authentication, to reduce the risk of key compromise leading to spoofing and tampering in consensus.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are vulnerable to compromise without proper management.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware security modules, enforce key rotation policies, and establish operational controls for key access.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management logs, HSM audit reports, and key rotation records.

checklist_items.title: Implement address-based authentication for proposal submissions
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance System requires proposal submitters to authenticate using their verified on-chain wallet address, linking submissions directly to a trusted identity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) relies on the address used at transaction submission to identify the proposer.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce proposal submissions only from verified, multisignature, or otherwise authenticated addresses.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing proposal submission from verified addresses with cryptographic signature verification.

checklist_items.title: Require digital signatures for RFRV proposal submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all RFRV proposals are accompanied by a cryptographic signature from the legitimate Berachain Foundation wallet, verifiable on-chain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposals from the Foundation may be spoofed if signatures are not verified.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed with the Foundation's private key, and verify signatures on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain verification logs showing signed proposal data matching public key signatures.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure off-chain proposal data submission channels
checklist_items.description: Use HTTPS, TLS, and cryptographic integrity checks for submission platforms to prevent tampering of proposal data during transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data can be tampered during transit due to insecure submission channels.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use HTTPS/TLS for all proposal submission interfaces and apply digital signatures for proposal data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Certificates, audit logs demonstrating encrypted submissions with verified signatures.

checklist_items.title: Require on-chain cryptographic proof for proposal content integrity
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain verification of proposal signatures to prevent tampering and impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data can be altered if not cryptographically verified after submission.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be submitted with a cryptographic signature verified on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction logs verifying signature validity for each proposal.

checklist_items.title: Establish strict access control for reward disbursement in smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Audit and verify that Reward Vaults enforce role-based access control to prevent unauthorized incentive offerings.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may have flawed access restrictions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and verify role-based access controls within Reward Vault contracts, using established patterns like OpenZeppelin's AccessControl.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and on-chain role assignment proofs.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature and operational security for Guardian keys
checklist_items.description: Ensure Guardian keys controlling proposal cancellation are stored securely, rotated regularly, and protected by multi-factor authentication.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig controlling Guardian actions may be vulnerable to key compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules (HSMs), enforce key rotation policies, and multi-party signature schemes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documents, audit logs demonstrating key rotations and access controls.

checklist_items.title: Enable detailed on-chain logging of guardian multisig actions
checklist_items.description: Configure multisig and governance contracts to emit events that record all signers and actions for proposal cancellations and other critical decisions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Current multisig implementations may lack detailed event logging for signers and actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive event emission on multisig transactions and governance actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs showing the list of signers and the completed actions.

checklist_items.title: Educate user base on secure private key management and verify transaction details in-wallet
checklist_items.description: Promote best practices for private key storage, use of hardware wallets, and meticulous verification of transaction parameters before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User private keys are vulnerable if not securely stored; user neglect can lead to compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct user education campaigns and provide user-friendly verification interfaces.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Educational material, user compliance reports, in-wallet transaction verification screenshots.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction signing confirmation prompts in wallets
checklist_items.description: Ensure wallets display detailed transaction information for user verification, minimizing risks of tampering or misrepresentation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not verify transaction details before signing, especially on malicious or compromised frontends.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enhance wallet UX to require explicit user confirmation of all transaction parameters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet UI logs capturing user confirmations, UI/UX review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Deploy anti-spam and rate limiting measures for RFRV submission platforms
checklist_items.description: Implement CAPTCHAs, submit fees, and rate limits on proposal submissions to prevent spam and overload of governance review.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal submission platform may lack rate limiting, enabling spam flooding.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use CAPTCHA challenges, submission fees, and implement throttling controls.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System logs demonstrating enforcement of rate limits and CAPTCHA completion.

checklist_items.title: Establish high availability infrastructure for Proposal Submission Platform
checklist_items.description: Deploy redundant servers, DDoS protections, and failover mechanisms to ensure continuous acceptance of RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal submission platform might be vulnerable to infrastructure-level outages or attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement load balancing, DDoS mitigation, and geo-redundant deployment.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Infrastructure architecture diagrams, incident response plans.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular security audits of smart contracts managing incentives and governance
checklist_items.description: Audit the Reward Vaults, governance, and PoL contracts for common vulnerabilities, including access control flaws, reentrancy, and logic errors.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may contain vulnerabilities that can be exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage third-party auditors, run formal verification, and implement bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification test results, bug bounty findings.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain role-based access control mechanisms in smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Use well-audited access control libraries and patterns to restrict critical functions to authorized roles.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may have flawed access control implementations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access controls using frameworks like OpenZeppelin's AccessControl.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports, on-chain role assignment data.

checklist_items.title: Implement threshold and multi-party signing for Proposal Cancellation
checklist_items.description: Require multiple Guardian signatures for proposal cancellations to prevent unilateral malicious actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig control for cancellations can be compromised if keys are mishandled.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, enforce key rotation, and require multiple signatures for cancellation actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction logs, key management policies.

checklist_items.title: Set up event logging for multisig guardian actions
checklist_items.description: Configure multisig and governance contracts to emit detailed events capturing signer identities and action details for audit purposes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Current multisig and governance contracts might lack sufficient logging for guardian actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement event emission for all multisig approval and cancellation actions with signer details.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs, analytics dashboards.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access control in Reward Vault smart contract to validate Token Manager identity.
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access control within the Reward Vault contract to ensure only designated Token Managers can add or modify incentives. This mitigates spoofing threats arising from private key compromise or contract vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control enforcement, withdrawal of rights for non-designated addresses.

checklist_items.title: Require explicit verification of transaction parameters when interacting via the wallet.
checklist_items.description: Advise Token Managers to manually verify all incentive parameter details (amount, rate, token address) in their wallets before signing transactions to prevent tampering due to compromised or malicious frontends.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Token Manager might not diligently verify all transaction parameters in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs showing token manager explicitly reviewing all transaction details prior to signing.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication and multi-signature requirements for governance proposal submissions.
checklist_items.description: Require proposals, especially from privileged addresses like the Foundation, to be signed with multi-factor authentication or multi-signature schemes to prevent spoofing and impersonation threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify the authenticity of the cancellation signal if a sufficient number of Guardian keys are compromised or the underlying multisig mechanism has a flaw.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Robust multisig implementation, secure key management practices for Guardians, monitoring of cancellation events, and potentially a delay/challenge period for cancellations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of multisig signers, signatures, and governance proposal submissions.

checklist_items.title: Audit and enforce hard-coded upper bounds on incentive rates in smart contracts.
checklist_items.description: Incorporate constraints in reward-setting functions to prevent setting excessively high incentive rates that could deplete the system's intended economic reserves or cause financial loss.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective hard-coded upper bounds or sanity checks in the smart contract for the incentive rate relative to the total amount of tokens deposited for that incentive.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Protocols should establish clear internal guidelines, review processes, and potentially multi-sig controls for setting/changing incentive rates. Consider implementing optional warnings or soft limits in any UI used by Token Managers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code audit reports indicating bounds checks exist for incentive rates.

checklist_items.title: Implement and verify on-chain identity verification for proposal submissions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all reward vault whitelisting proposals (RFRV) are submitted by verified on-chain identities to prevent impersonation or spoofing, as Assumption 'The RFRV review process may not verify proposer identity.' suggests.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV review process may not verify the true identity of the submitter.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signature or unique on-chain identity verification for proposal submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal data with verified identity proof.

checklist_items.title: Enforce cryptographic signing of the final proposal payload by the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering of submission data, the Foundation should sign the complete proposal payload, and the Governance System must verify this signature, aligning with mitigation 'Ensure end-to-end integrity protection.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal data is vulnerable to modification before being recorded.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signatures on the entire proposal payload and verify on submission.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal data and verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Ensure multi-factor authentication and secure key management for Guardians.
checklist_items.description: To prevent impersonation of Guardians, implement strong operational security, key management, and multisig procedures, as mitigation 'Rigorous security audits of the Guardian multisig contract.' suggests.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or process for Guardian management is vulnerable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure multisig setups with key control, regular audits, and operational security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing multisig operations, key management protocols.

checklist_items.title: Implement gas limits and design to prevent smart contract DoS vectors.
checklist_items.description: According to mitigation 'Conduct security audits for DoS vulnerabilities,' ensure that deployment of reward vault and other contracts have gas constraints, and avoid unbounded loops or logic that can cause resource exhaustion.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts, including Reward Vaults, can contain bugs leading to reverts or resource exhaustion.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure development practices, audits, and implement gas constraints and circuit breakers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, gas analysis records.

checklist_items.title: Establish operator and key management best practices for Validator nodes.
checklist_items.description: To prevent spoofing of consensus messages, implement 'robust key management practices for validators (e.g., HSMs, key rotation)' as mitigation in 'Implement robust key management practices for validators.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator has the technical capability to construct and modify block contents before proposal.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules, regular key rotations, and monitoring of validator activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports, key management logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHA, and spam filtering for proposals submissions.
checklist_items.description: Mitigate spam or denial of service via excessive proposal submissions by enforcing rate limits, CAPTCHA, or submission fees, as suggested by 'Implement...rate limiting and filtering.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective rate-limiting mechanisms for proposal creation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure submission portals with rate limits, CAPTCHA, and submission fees.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Submission logs showing rate-limiting enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Verify transaction parameters against trusted, signed payloads before execution.
checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering in wallet interactions such as swaps or reward claims, ensure transaction parameters match signed, verified data, as per 'Implement strong frontend security.' and 'Users should carefully verify transaction details.'
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not meticulously verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement transaction sign-off verification tools and educate users.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User confirmation logs, transaction analysis reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement and verify that Reward Vault smart contracts authenticate the caller as the designated Token Manager address for incentive management functions.
checklist_items.description: The security gap indicates a potential flaw in Reward Vault contracts' access control for incentive management functions. Ensuring proper authentication prevents spoofing of Token Manager identities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring only designated Token Managers can execute incentive functions. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control enforcement, smart contract source code with access modifiers.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure access control modifiers are rigorously tested and verified in the smart contract code.

checklist_items.title: Verify that transaction parameters for setting incentive rates are meticulously checked and verified within the user's wallet or interaction interface, especially when interacting through the dApp frontend.
checklist_items.description: The gap indicates that incentive parameters such as rate and deposit amount could be tampered with if the user does not verify transaction details before signing, especially when using compromised or malicious interfaces.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, always verify transaction details meticulously before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of verified transaction details in a trusted wallet or audit logs of verified interactions.
checklist_items.notes: Encourage users to review transaction details carefully to prevent tampering.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure, multi-factor authentication mechanisms for all proposal submission interfaces to prevent spoofing of Reward Vault whitelisting proposals from the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.description: Since the threat involves impersonation or spoofing of the Foundation's proposals, strong authentication mechanisms are necessary to verify proposal origins and prevent malicious submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing multi-factor authentication steps during proposal submission.
checklist_items.notes: Regular audits and operational security procedures for Foundation proposal submissions are essential.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all Reward Vault whitelisting proposals are cryptographically signed by the Berachain Foundation's authorized signing keys, and verify signatures during on-chain processing.
checklist_items.description: This prevents tampering during the proposal submission process and provides non-repudiation and integrity for Foundation proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal data is vulnerable to modification after being sent by the Berachain Foundation but before being recorded, if integrity checks are insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require Foundation to sign the complete and final proposal payload with their private key, and verify signatures during on-chain processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract verification logs and off-chain signatures used during proposal submission.
checklist_items.notes: Use standard cryptographic signature standards (e.g., ECDSA) for proposal signatures.

checklist_items.title: Enforce rate-limiting, CAPTCHA, and anti-spam measures on the RFRV submission platform to prevent spam and flooding attacks.
checklist_items.description: High volume of fake or low-value RFRV submissions can overwhelm the review process, leading to denial of legitimate proposal submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective rate-limiting or anti-spam measures for RFRV channels.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement CAPTCHAs, submission fees, rate limiting, and filtering criteria on the RFRV submission platform.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System logs showing submission rate and filter effectiveness.
checklist_items.notes: Balance security measures with usability to avoid discouraging legitimate proposals.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-party multisignature control with threshold signatures for all governance proposals and proposal cancellations, including guardian actions, to prevent unauthorized or malicious act spoofing or elevation.
checklist_items.description: Weak multisig controls or key management flaws could allow attacker to spoof guardian approval signals or unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or the governance process does not log or make easily queryable which signers approved actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use well-audited multisig contracts, enforce secure key management, and require multiple approvals for proposals and cancellations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain multisig event logs containing signer signatures.
checklist_items.notes: Regularly audit the multisig governance mechanisms and key controls.

checklist_items.title: Develop and implement a robust incident response plan for validator key compromise, including key rotation, immediate slashing, and forensic analysis procedures.
checklist_items.description: The risk of validator key compromise leading to spoofing or malicious consensus actions requires preparedness and operational controls.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys used for signing consensus messages are vulnerable to compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish procedures for rapid key revocation, rotation, and incident forensic analysis.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Incident response plans, logs of key revocations, and rotation records.
checklist_items.notes: Regular key rotation and multi-factor security for validator keys are essential.

checklist_items.title: Ensure validators' software includes cryptographic proofs and consistency checks to detect and report any tampering with proposed blocks or consensus messages.
checklist_items.description: Implement network-level verification and monitoring to detect inconsistencies, unauthorized modifications, and malicious behavior.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validators have the capacity to detect and verify the correctness of proposed blocks and consensus messages.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use cryptographic proofs, chain validation, and consensus monitoring tools.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validator operational logs and cryptographic proof verification reports.
checklist_items.notes: Regular audits and network monitoring are crucial to detect malicious tampering.

checklist_items.title: Implement and enforce strong on-chain access controls for Reward Vault incentive management functions including add, offer, and modify incentives
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only designated Token Managers can execute incentive management functions within Reward Vault contracts, verifying that private keys are securely managed and stored.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute incentive functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control enforcement, smart contract code with access modifiers.

checklist_items.title: Require secure, trusted interfaces and client-side verification for interacting with incentive parameter-setting functions
checklist_items.description: Token Managers should use secure and audited interfaces (dApps or wallet integrations) to set incentive parameters, and meticulously verify all transaction details before signing. Avoid interaction via potentially compromised frontends.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always verify parameters before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots, transaction logs showing verification process.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction parameter verification and confirmation prompts in user wallets for all interaction with smart contracts that modify incentive settings or rewards
checklist_items.description: Require users to verify and confirm transaction details explicitly in their wallets (preferably hardware wallets) before signing transactions that alter incentive or reward configurations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might not diligently verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Wallets should display all transaction parameters clearly, and users should verify these details before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet transaction confirmation records, UI/UX documentation.

checklist_items.title: Establish and monitor capacity limits and resource thresholds for Reward Vaults to prevent denial of service due to resource exhaustion or contract limitations
checklist_items.description: Deploy smart contract checks and operational policies to ensure Reward Vaults do not exceed capacity limits for incentive types, and monitor gas usage and token balances to prevent resource depletion.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults have a functional limit on the number of distinct active incentive types, and contract bugs or resource shortages can cause management failures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement in-contract caps and thresholds, monitor gas and token levels, and set up alerts for resource exhaustion.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring logs, cap enforcement code snippets.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict update and reward distribution rules with formal verification of reward allocation logic within validator consensus contracts
checklist_items.description: Verify that smart contracts handling validator reward allocations (e.g., reward percentages, vault selection) include formal constraints or hardcoded limits, and undergo formal verification and audits to prevent manipulation or misconfiguration.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are specific constraints on reward allocations, but potential logic flaws could be exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct formal verification and rigorous security audits of reward distribution code, with emphasis on boundary enforcement.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Formal verification reports, audit statements.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication and operational procedures for proposal submissions from the Berachain Foundation to prevent spoofing and impersonation attacks
checklist_items.description: Require proposals from the Foundation to be signed with multiple keys, use verified identity mechanisms, and undergo multi-party approval before submission.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System might not verify the legitimacy of Foundation proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multisig, cryptographic signatures, and verified onboarding procedures for Foundation proposal submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Proposal submission logs with multi-signature verification details.

checklist_items.title: Require cryptographic signing and signature verification of all proposal data, including the content of Reward Vault whitelisting proposals
checklist_items.description: Each Proposal submitted by the Foundation should be signed with a private key, and the governance system must verify this signature to ensure integrity and authenticity of proposal data.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data could be altered if not properly signed and verified.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement digital signature schemes for all proposal data, and verify signatures before acceptance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs, signed proposal files.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access controls within Reward Vault smart contracts to ensure only designated Token Managers can modify incentives.
checklist_items.description: Given the security gap where Reward Vault contracts may not correctly authenticate the caller as the legitimate Token Manager, enforce access control checks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing only authorized Token Manager addresses invoke incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require all governance proposals to be signed with the Berachain Foundation's verified multi-signature wallet, and verify signatures on-chain before processing.
checklist_items.description: To prevent spoofed proposals from the Foundation, proposals should have cryptographic signatures verified on-chain to ensure authenticity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) authenticates proposals from the Berachain Foundation based on a mechanism (e.g., signature from a specific EOA or multisig).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures. The system should verify signatures cryptographically on-chain before accepting proposals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal payloads and on-chain verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels (e.g., HTTPS, signed messages) between the Foundation's proposal submission platform and the Governance System to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate tampering of the proposal data during transit, use cryptographic signatures or secure transport protocols.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal data is vulnerable to modification after being sent by the Berachain Foundation but before being recorded by the Governance System.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure submission channels (e.g., HTTPS, cryptographically signed messages), implement data integrity checks such as checksums or digital signatures, and verify signatures upon receipt.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal data with verification logs demonstrating integrity checks.

checklist_items.title: Develop and deploy a comprehensive monitoring system to detect unauthorized activity or anomalies in validator key usage and validator behavior.
checklist_items.description: Monitoring validator activity can help detect compromise or malicious behavior that could lead to spoofing or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management practices, monitor validator signing activity for anomalies, and deploy slashing mechanisms for misbehavior.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validator activity logs and anomaly reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement role-based access control for Reward Vault incentive addition functions
checklist_items.description: Ensure only designated Token Managers can add or offer incentives in the Reward Vault to prevent spoofing or unauthorized actions, based on the mitigation strategy for threat ID 1.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring only designated Token Managers can execute these functions. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control functions restrict actions to approved Token Manager addresses.

checklist_items.title: Enforce transaction parameter verification in incentive management interfaces
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all transactions setting incentive rates or depositing tokens in the Reward Vault are verified for correctness before signing, to prevent tampering as described in threat ID 2.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious interface to manipulate transaction parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure, audited interfaces and verify all transaction details meticulously before signing. Wallets should clearly display parameters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots or logs of transaction details verified in a trusted wallet before signing.

checklist_items.title: Configure account security measures to prevent Guardian key compromise and unauthorized multisig signatures
checklist_items.description: Implement secure key management practices, multi-factor authentication, and regular key rotation for Guardians to prevent high-impact spoofing or elevation of privilege attacks as per threat ID 11.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or the Guardian key management process has vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct rigorous security audits of multisig implementations, enforce secure key management, and establish operational procedures for key rotation and oversight.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of multisig security and operational procedures; key management logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure communication channels for off-chain proposal and cancellation signals
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all off-chain communications involving Guardian signatures or proposal cancellations use cryptographic signatures and secure channels to prevent tampering, directly addressing threat ID 6.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The cancellation signal relies on an off-chain relay that might be vulnerable to interception or modification.

checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration details showing use of signatures and secure channels for proposal/cancellation submissions.

checklist_items.title: Ensure on-chain proposal submission verification with cryptographic signatures
checklist_items.description: Require all governance proposals and RFRV submissions to be cryptographically signed by the submitter's verified identity to prevent spoofing or repudiation, as addressed in threat IDs 21 and 23.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposals are vulnerable to spoofing if not properly signed and verified.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate digital signatures from the submitter using a verified identity key and verify these signatures on-chain before processing the proposals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain records showing verified signatures accompany each proposal submission.
