Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Record of verified transaction details confirmed by the Token Manager prior to signing.

checklist_items.title: Ensure reward allocation modifications are transparent and auditable.
checklist_items.description: Require all reward allocation changes to be logged with detailed signer information and timestamps, enabling accountability and preventing elevation of privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or the Governance System does not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure the multisig and Governance System emit detailed events logging all individual signers for critical actions like proposal cancellation. Maintain immutable and accessible on-chain records.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Event logs showing individual signers for each governance action.

checklist_items.title: Require proposals to be signed with verifiable cryptographic signatures from the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.description: All proposal submissions should be accompanied by a cryptographic signature from the Foundation's authorized signing key, which the Governance System verifies before processing.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures. Regularly audit access and authorization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Cryptographic proof of signature verifying the Foundation’s authority on each proposal.

checklist_items.title: Verify the integrity of off-chain proposal data during submission.
checklist_items.description: Use secure submission platforms with end-to-end encryption and data integrity checks (e.g., digital signatures, checksums) to prevent tampering of Reward Vault whitelisting proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The whitelisting proposal data submitted by the Berachain Foundation (Actor 8) could be altered by an attacker before being finalized by the Governance System (Component 29) if integrity checks are insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure submission channels (e.g., HTTPS for forms), implement data integrity checks (e.g., checksums, digital signatures for submissions), and ensure secure storage on the submission platform.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs and cryptographic signatures confirming data integrity during submission.

checklist_items.title: Verify that the Reward Vault contract enforces strict access control to only allow designated Token Managers to add or modify incentives.

checklist_items.title: Establish and enforce on-chain constraints to prevent reward allocation settings from exceeding system-defined limits (e.g., total allocation <= 100%), avoid individual excesses, and validate parameters during configuration.
checklist_items.description: Add on-chain checks in reward configuration functions to ensure that the sum of reward allocations and individual vault caps do not surpass system-defined thresholds, preventing tampering or misconfigurations.

checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing validation logic and on-chain constraints in reward allocation functions.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor, role-based access control (RBAC) and conduct periodic security audits on the Guardian multisig control mechanisms to prevent unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.description: Utilize multi-signature requirements with multiple independent Guardians, enforce secure key management, and periodically review access rights to prevent unauthorized cancellation actions.

checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction logs, key management audit reports, and event logs showing individual Guardian approvals.

Strong key management and operational security for all legitimate Guardians. Multi-factor authentication for any administrative actions on the multisig if applicable.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Reward Vault and associated contracts are thoroughly audited

checklist_items.description: Require multiple Guardians or an operational multi-signature process for submitting and approving Reward Vault whitelisting proposals to prevent spoofing and unauthorized submissions.

checklist_items.title: Verify cryptographic signatures for all proposals and submissions from the Foundation
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all Reward Vault whitelisting proposals and RFRVs are signed with the Foundation's private keys, and signature verification is enforced during on-chain or off-chain review.

checklist_items.title: Implement end-to-end integrity verification for proposal data submissions to prevent tampering during transit.

checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use trusted wallet software, verify all transaction details before signing, and utilize security best practices in dApp frontend development.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots or logs of transaction parameter verification prior to signing.

checklist_items.title: Establish clear internal guidelines and review processes for setting incentive parameters
checklist_items.description: Define and enforce protocol-level guidelines and multi-signature approval for critical incentive parameter changes, including incentive rates like Distribution Per BGT.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Protocols should establish clear internal guidelines, review processes, and potentially multi-sig controls for setting/changing incentive rates.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-signature controls for incentive rate adjustments, establish internal review procedures, and configure optional warnings or soft limits in related UI/UX.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Records of multi-signature approvals or review logs.

checklist_items.title: Enable comprehensive monitoring and alerting for Reward Vault capacity and contract health
checklist_items.description: Set up monitoring systems to track the number of active incentive types, vault capacity, and contract health metrics to prevent capacity-based denial of incentive management functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults may reach maximum capacity or contain bugs that revert legitimate transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy monitoring solutions that track vault capacity, transaction success rates, and contract error logs; set alerts for thresholds.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboard screenshots, alerts logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain signature verification for proposals submitted by the Berachain Foundation
checklist_items.description: Require proposals from the Foundation to be signed with a cryptographically verified key, and verify the signature on-chain to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify the authenticity of the proposal origin.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require that proposal submissions include a cryptographic signature from a known, verified Foundation address; implement on-chain signature verification logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction data and signature verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Secure member management of the Guardian multisig through rigorous audits and operational controls
checklist_items.description: Conduct regular security audits of the Guardian multisig mechanism and establish operational procedures including offline key storage, multi-factor authentication, and secure key rotation to prevent unauthorized control over proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig contract (implied, as Guardians operate via multisig) has a vulnerability in its member management functions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rigorous security audits, secure key storage (HSMs), operational key management procedures, and multi-factor authentication for administrative actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, operational procedures documentation.

checklist_items.title: Ensure multisig signatures for proposal cancellations are comprehensively logged with individual signer attribution
checklist_items.description: Configure the multisig contract and governance system to emit detailed events that log each Guardian's signature during proposal cancellation, enabling accountability and auditability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or governance system does not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement event logging that records individual signer addresses for each multisig operation, and ensure logs are stored immutably.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Event logs demonstrating signer attribution for proposal cancellation transactions.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signing and verification for RFRV proposals to prevent tampering
checklist_items.description: Require that all Reward Vault whitelisting proposal data be signed with a cryptographic key from the applicant, and verify this signature within the governance process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: End-to-end integrity protection for all submitted proposals is necessary.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposal submissions to include a digital signature from a verified key, and implement on-chain signature verification logic to accept only signed data with valid signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Proposal data and signature verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels and operational security protocols for Guardian off-chain discussions
checklist_items.description: Create guidelines and technical measures (e.g., encrypted messaging, secure portals) for Guardians to discuss sensitive proposal or cancellation details, reducing the risk of information disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians discuss potential cancellations or proposal details off-chain before executing any on-chain action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure communication channels, operational security policies, and training for Guardians on protecting off-chain communications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational policies, Guardian training records, communication platform security assessments.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only the designated Token Manager can offer or modify incentives.
checklist_items.description: The Reward Vault contract's access control must verify that only the legitimate Token Manager address can add, offer, or manage incentives. This prevents spoofing or unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control checks for incentive management functions; code review reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure transaction verification procedures for Token Managers, including meticulous parameter verification before signing transactions.
checklist_items.description: Token Managers should use secure, audited interfaces and verify all transaction parameters meticulously in their wallet before signing to prevent parameter tampering or tampering via compromised interfaces.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of verified transaction details; audit reports of wallet interfaces.

checklist_items.title: Require direct on-chain submission of governance proposals from verified, multisignature-controlled addresses to prevent spoofed proposals.
checklist_items.description: All governance proposals, including Reward Vault whitelisting, should originate directly from addresses controlled by verified multisignature wallets managed by the Berachain Foundation, with signatures validated on-chain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) authenticates proposals from the Berachain Foundation based on a mechanism (e.g., signature from a specific EOA or multisig).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce submission of proposals via a verified multisignature wallet with multi-factor authentication and signature verification on-chain. Regularly audit multisig signers and control procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain proposal submission logs; multisig transaction signatures.

checklist_items.title: Implement end-to-end integrity verification for all RFRV proposal data submissions, including digital signatures and cryptographic checksums.
checklist_items.description: The proposal data submitted for reward vault whitelisting must be signed by the originator’s private key and verified upon receipt in the Governance System to prevent tampering during transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The whitelisting proposal data submitted by the Berachain Foundation (Actor 8) could be altered by an attacker before being finalized by the Governance System.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require all proposal data to be signed with the submitter's private key; verify signatures upon receipt and before processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Cryptographic signature verification logs; signed proposal files.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels for off-chain proposal data, including encryption and integrity checks, to prevent tampering during transit.
checklist_items.description: All off-chain proposal data, including submission payloads and communication with the Governance System, must be protected via encryption and integrity verification mechanisms.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The whitelisting proposal data is vulnerable to modification after being sent by the Berachain Foundation but before being recorded by the Governance System.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure channels such as HTTPS with TLS 1.3, implement digital signatures for data authenticity, and maintain audit logs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Encrypted transmission logs; signature verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and anti-spam controls for RFRV proposal submission interfaces.
checklist_items.description: The proposal submission platform should enforce rate limits, CAPTCHA challenges, or submission fees to prevent spam floods that can cause denial of service or overwhelm the review process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV submission process may not have robust mechanisms to verify the true identity and legitimacy of every submitting project/developer.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Incorporate CAPTCHA, submission fees, and rate limiting. Monitor submission patterns and flag suspicious activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Server logs showing submission rates; CAPTCHA verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Use hardware security modules and operational security best practices to protect Guardian private keys used for multisig operations.
checklist_items.description: Guardians' keys must be stored in secure hardware modules, with operational security measures such as key rotation, multi-factor access controls, and offline storage to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig implementation and key management are critical for preventing unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy hardware security modules (HSMs); enforce key rotation policies; limit access to multisig signing keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access; HSM deployment verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive monitoring and alerting systems for Guardian multisig activities, including proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.description: All multisig proposal cancellations should be logged on-chain with detailed signer information and monitored for anomalies or unauthorized actions. Alerts should trigger on suspicious patterns.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians involved in a proposal cancellation might attempt to deny their actions or dispute involvement.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Set up on-chain event logging with signer attribution; enable real-time monitoring; define thresholds for abnormal activity and alert accordingly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs; monitoring dashboards.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure, off-chain communication channels with end-to-end encryption for Guardian operations involving proposal cancellation signals.
checklist_items.description: Any off-chain relay or communication of Guardian signatures or cancellation signals must employ cryptographic encryption and integrity protection to prevent tampering or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Tampering with a legitimate cancellation signal from Guardians during an off-chain relay can cause incorrect or malicious proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use end-to-end encrypted communication protocols; sign transaction data; validate signatures on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Encrypted communication logs; signature verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Maintain detailed, immutable, and accessible on-chain logs of multisig Guardian signers and approval events.
checklist_items.description: All multisig proposal cancellation actions should be recorded in on-chain logs with individual signer identities and timestamps, enabling accountability and dispute resolution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians involved in a proposal cancellation might attempt to deny their actions or dispute involvement.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design smart contracts to emit detailed events with signer identities and timestamps for all multisig actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs; audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Reward Vault smart contract enforces strict access control for incentive management functions
checklist_items.description: Implement access control within the Reward Vault contract to ensure only authorized Token Managers can add, offer, or manage incentives. Verify that privilege checks are robust and cannot be bypassed.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating access control enforcement during incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require multisig approval with threshold signatures for governance proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Implement multisignature approval mechanisms for proposal cancellations during timelock, requiring at least the defined threshold of Guardian signatures to authorize cancellations. Regularly audit multisig configuration and monitor for suspicious activity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig implementation has vulnerabilities or insufficient operational security, allowing spoofed cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Utilize well-audited multisig contracts with strict signer management, conduct regular security audits, and implement operational procedures for Guardian key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain events with detailed signer signatures for each proposal cancellation.

checklist_items.title: Verify proposal submissions are signed with cryptographically verified signatures from the Berachain Foundation
checklist_items.description: Require proposal submissions to be accompanied by a cryptographic signature from the Foundation's trusted signing key, and verify the signature on-chain before accepting the proposal. This ensures authenticity and prevents spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System does not verify proposal origin authenticity, risking spoofed submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement signature verification for all proposal submissions, utilizing cryptographic signatures from verified Foundation keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain verification logs showing proposal signatures matching Foundation's public key.

checklist_items.title: Enforce end-to-end integrity checks for off-chain proposal data submissions
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographic signatures or checksums to ensure that proposal data submitted via off-chain channels has not been tampered with during transit. Verify signatures on-chain before processing proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data can be altered during transmission if not secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposers to sign proposal data with their private key or a shared secret, and verify the signatures on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification reports showing proposal data integrity.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain, cryptographically verifiable proof of proposal submission origin
checklist_items.description: Design proposal submission process to include a cryptographic proof linking the submission to the identity of the Foundation or authorized submitter, such as signed messages or verified addresses. Use this proof during proposal processing to prevent repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The on-chain transaction record can be claimed by the Foundation as compromised, undermining non-repudiation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require signed messages from trusted Foundation addresses, stored and verified on-chain for each proposal submission.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain logs of signed proposal submissions with verifiable signatures from Foundation keys.

checklist_items.title: Configure proposal submission platform with secure, encrypted channels and strong access controls
checklist_items.description: Ensure that off-chain proposal submission methods (e.g., web forms, email) use HTTPS/TLS, access controls, and do not accept unauthenticated or unverified input to prevent tampering in transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data can be intercepted or modified during transit to the review platform.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure, encrypted submission channels with rigorous access controls, and implement data integrity checks such as signatures or checksums.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network logs showing secure transmission of proposal data.

checklist_items.title: Establish strict proposal creation limits and rate-limiting mechanisms to prevent spam
checklist_items.description: Implement deposit requirements, rate-limiting, and CAPTCHA verifications on proposal creation interfaces to minimize spam submissions and ensure system availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal creation process is vulnerable to spam and flooding.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce minimum deposit requirements, rate limits, and CAPTCHA verifications, and monitor submission patterns for abuse.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of proposal submissions with timestamps and deposit info.

checklist_items.title: Secure validator key management with hardware security modules and regular rotation
checklist_items.description: Store validator private keys within HSMs or similar secure hardware, enforce strict operational procedures, and rotate keys periodically. Monitor for signs of key compromise or misuse.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's key compromise risks enable spoofing of validator actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules, implement key rotation policies, and conduct regular key management audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management logs and audit reports showing secure storage and rotation procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication for validator administrative controls
checklist_items.description: Require multi-factor authentication for any administrative actions or key management operations related to validator keys. Use hardware tokens or similar 2FA methods.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Improper access control or weak authentication for validator management can lead to unauthorized key operations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce MFA for all admin actions, especially key rotations and operator privileges.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs showing MFA used during administrative operations.

checklist_items.title: Regularly monitor validator activity logs for suspicious or anomalous behavior
checklist_items.description: Set up continuous monitoring on validator activity logs to detect signs of key compromise, unauthorized actions, or abnormal participation rates, and promptly respond to incidents.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator operations may include malicious or compromised activity if keys are misused.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy activity monitoring, anomaly detection systems, and incident response procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Alert logs and audit trails indicating abnormal validator behavior.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction signing verification with cryptographic signatures before proposal or action submission
checklist_items.description: Require all proposal and governance actions to include on-chain verification of cryptographic signatures from authorized signers. Reject transactions lacking valid signatures from verified identities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal submissions can be spoofed without signature verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed with private keys of authorized Foundation addresses and verify signatures before acceptance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain signature verification logs showing valid signatures from Foundation keys.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only the designated Token Manager address can add or manage incentives.
checklist_items.description: The Reward Vault contract's current logic might not correctly authenticate the caller as the legitimate Token Manager, risking spoofing threats for incentives management.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or contract source code verifying access control mechanisms.

checklist_items.title: Require users to verify all transaction details in a trusted wallet interface before signing, and implement frontend security best practices such as Content Security Policy (CSP) and Subresource Integrity (SRI).
checklist_items.description: User's interaction with the BEX frontend for liquidity provision is susceptible to tampering if the frontend is compromised or malicious.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The dApp frontend is susceptible to compromise (e.g., XSS, DNS hijacking, malicious code injection) or is intentionally malicious.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Users must meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing, and frontend developers should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI). Wallets should clearly display all critical parameters for verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction review screens, code review reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure, access-controlled channels for submitting Reward Vault whitelisting proposals and implement robust vetting procedures by the Governance System.
checklist_items.description: The Berachain Foundation's proposal submission can be compromised or delayed due to insecure channels or DoS attacks, risking ecosystem governance progress.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Foundation's infrastructure for proposal submission can be targeted and disrupted.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement resilient infrastructure, rate limiting, and anomaly detection mechanisms on proposal submission channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network logs showing proposal submission success rates, incident response reports.

checklist_items.title: Utilize multisignature wallets with multi-factor authentication and conduct regular audits for the Guardian multisig contract managing proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.description: Compromise of Guardian keys can lead to unauthorized proposal cancellations, undermining governance integrity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig implementation may have vulnerabilities or operational security issues.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use strong multisig schemes, enforce key management best practices, and conduct regular security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction logs, audit reports, key management policies.

checklist_items.title: Implement cross-chain or on-chain proposal signatures from the Berachain Foundation to prevent spoofing of Proposal submissions.
checklist_items.description: Proposal submissions from the Foundation can be spoofed if the submission process lacks cryptographic verification, leading to malicious proposals being accepted.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not verify the authenticity of proposals based on cryptographic signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals from the Foundation to be signed with their private keys, and verify signatures on-chain before processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal payloads, verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish end-to-end integrity protections for Off-Chain proposal data, including signing proposal payloads and verifying signatures during processing.
checklist_items.description: Tampering with proposal data in transit can lead to malicious modifications before on-chain recording or review.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data is vulnerable to modification during transit or storage unless protected.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signatures for all proposal data, and verify signatures upon receipt.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal data, verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate-limiting, CAPTCHA challenges, and platform verification for RFRV submissions to prevent spam and denial of service attacks.
checklist_items.description: Excessive or fake RFRV submissions can overwhelm the review process, delaying genuine proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: No effective spam control measures are in place for RFRV submission channels.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use CAPTCHA, impose submission limits, and require deposit fees.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Submission logs, spam detection reports.

checklist_items.title: Require all proposal and transaction actions in the wallet to be cross-verified and displayed before signing, especially for critical governance or reward operations.
checklist_items.description: Users signing without verifying transaction details risk executing tampered actions intended by a compromised frontend or malicious software.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users rely on the accuracy of displayed transaction details before signing; compromised frontends can alter data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Display comprehensive transaction summaries in wallets, use hardware wallets with their own verification mechanisms, and educate users.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User confirmation records, wallet verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Develop and enforce a security policy for private key storage, including hardware wallet use and offline backups, to prevent private key disclosure.
checklist_items.description: Compromise of private keys leads to total loss and potential repudiation of actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys/seed phrases are stored insecurely or shared among untrusted applications.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallet adoption, offline seed phrase backups, and secure key management practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, hardware wallet verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit smart contracts involved in the Mint and Redeem processes for vulnerabilities, including backdoors, overloaded functions, or access control flaws.
checklist_items.description: Exploitable vulnerabilities can allow unauthorized minting or redeeming of $HONEY, leading to inflation or asset theft.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contract code may contain vulnerabilities despite audits.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct multiple external audits, formal verification, and participate in bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, bug bounty findings.

checklist_items.title: Require proposal submissions from the decoder or submitter to be signed with their verified on-chain identity or platform account to prevent repudiation and identity spoofing.
checklist_items.description: Without cryptographic proof linking the submitter to the statement, malicious actors can deny responsibility for fraudulent proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data is not cryptographically signed or linked to verified identities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signature by the submitter, verified against known identities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal payloads, verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement and enforce strong access control in Reward Vault contracts to ensure only designated Token Managers can manage incentives
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized Token Manager addresses can execute functions to add, offer, or manage incentives in the Reward Vault, preventing spoofing of incentive offerings.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating access controls are enforced and only designated addresses can modify incentives.

checklist_items.title: Require all governance proposals, including Reward Vault whitelisting, to be submitted with cryptographic signatures from the Berachain Foundation’s verified address
checklist_items.description: Implement cryptographic signature verification within the governance contract to authenticate that proposals originate from the legitimate Foundation address, preventing spoofing or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal submission process may not sufficiently verify the true origin of proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed by the Foundation's private key and verify signatures upon receipt in the governance contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Cryptographic signatures accompanying proposal data, with verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Enhance off-chain proposal submission channels with end-to-end encryption and integrity checks
checklist_items.description: Utilize secure communication protocols (e.g., HTTPS with TLS 1.3, digital signatures) for proposal submissions to prevent tampering during transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data is vulnerable to modification after being sent by the Foundation but before being recorded.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure, encrypted submission channels with digital signatures from submitters to ensure data integrity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit trail of submission process showing secure transport and signature verification.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain logging of multisig Guardian actions with detailed signer attribution
checklist_items.description: Ensure that every Guardian multisig transaction, especially proposal cancellations, emits on-chain events logging each signer's identity to enable accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance System or multisig contract does not log or make accessible the list of signers for each action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design multisig contracts to emit detailed event logs capturing each signer’s identity for critical actions like proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs showing signer addresses for each multisig action.

checklist_items.title: Require proposal submission from the Foundation to be signed with a verifiable multi-signature from multiple authorized Guardians
checklist_items.description: Use multi-signature schemes with threshold signatures for proposal submissions from the Foundation, reducing risk of a single compromised key enabling impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal submission process can be spoofed if single private keys are used.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate multi-signature approval from multiple Guardians before proposal submission is accepted.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Proposal submission records including multi-signature verification across Guardian keys.

checklist_items.title: Set static or soft limits for incentive rates and implement real-time sanity checks within smart contracts to prevent excessive incentive setting
checklist_items.description: In the Reward Vault or incentive management contracts, implement maximum allowable incentive rates and checks to verify that rates are within acceptable bounds before accepting changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Prize for misconfiguration of incentive rate can lead to rapid depletion. No hard bounds currently exist.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish set maximum incentive rates and implement code to enforce these bounds during transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing rate settings with checks applied.

checklist_items.title: Require all validator key operations (proposing, signing, setting reward allocations) to be protected by hardware security modules (HSMs) or Multi-Party Computation (MPC) arrangements
checklist_items.description: Secure validator operational keys using hardware security modules or MPC standards to prevent unauthorized signature generation or key compromise, reducing spoofing and elevation risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator keys are vulnerable if stored insecurely or insufficiently protected.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy validator keys within HSMs or MPC fragments with strict operational security protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management system audit logs and hardware isolation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication and access controls for all validator key management and governance administrative actions
checklist_items.description: Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) and strict access control policies for validator key creation and governance parameter adjustments, preventing unauthorized elevation or spoofing of validator authority.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unauthorized access to validator or governance keys is possible if controls are weak.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate MFA and role-based access controls for all critical key management interfaces.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs showing MFA prompts and authorization events.

checklist_items.title: Configure API endpoints and RPC interfaces for validator nodes with rate limiting, IP whitelisting, and endpoint security measures
checklist_items.description: Prevent abuse or impersonation of validator operations by implementing rate limiting, IP whitelisting, and secure API practices on node RPC and management interfaces.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator RPC interfaces might be exposed to public networks vulnerable to abuse.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure API gateways with rate limiting, IP filtering, and TLS.
checklist_items.evidence_required: API access logs demonstrating rate limits and IP restrictions.

checklist_items.title: Regularly conduct smart contract security audits and formal verification for all critical contract code, especially reward distribution, stake, and governance contracts
checklist_items.description: Engage specialized security auditors to review code for logical flaws, vulnerabilities, and compliance with security best practices, reducing tampering and elevation risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts handling rewards or governance may contain vulnerabilities exploitable by malicious actors.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Commission periodic audits and formal verification processes for critical smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proofs or reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish an incident response plan for validator key compromise or malicious activity, including prompt key revocation and key rotation procedures
checklist_items.description: Prepare formal incident response procedures to respond rapidly to validator key compromise, including revoking suspicious keys, rotating keys, and updating validator configurations securely.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Compromise of validator keys can be exploited to spoof validator actions or disrupt consensus.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Develop incident response playbooks, train staff, and automate key rotation where possible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Incident response plan documentation and logs of key rotation activities.

checklist_items.title: Implement access control within the Reward Vault contract to ensure that only designated Token Managers can add or modify incentives.
checklist_items.description: The security gap indicates that the Reward Vault contract might not correctly authenticate Token Managers. Implementing strict access controls ensures only legitimate addresses can manage incentives.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control enforcement and approval records for incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-signature authorization for setting incentive rates or deposit parameters in the reward management interface.
checklist_items.description: Mitigates tampering risks where a compromised or malicious interface could alter incentive parameters before transaction confirmation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing multi-signature approval records for incentive parameter changes.

checklist_items.title: Establish on-chain or off-chain signing verification for Deposit/Set Incentive transactions submitted to Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.description: Validates that incentive management actions are authorized by legitimate Token Managers, preventing spoofing during parameters updates.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might not diligently verify all transaction details or could be spoofed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require all incentive management transactions to be signed with a secure, hardware-backed, or multi-signature wallet setup, and verify signatures before execution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Cryptographic signature verification logs and audit trail of authorized transactions.

checklist_items.title: Implement governance proposal signing and verification to prevent spoofed proposals from unauthorized sources.
checklist_items.description: Ensures that whitelisting proposals for Reward Vaults are cryptographically verified and originate from authorized entities like the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify the authenticity of the proposal source.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed with the Foundation's private key, with signature verification on-chain to confirm origin before processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal data with on-chain verification records.

checklist_items.title: Configure and enforce strict access controls for Guardians involved in proposal cancellations, including multi-factor authentication and key management best practices.
checklist_items.description: Reduces the risk of spoofed or unauthorized multisig cancellation signals, mitigating the threat of compromised Guardian keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig contract (implied) has a vulnerability in its member management functions or signature validation logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Perform rigorous security audits of the multisig contract; enforce secure key management, hardware security modules, and multi-factor authentication for Guardian operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, multisig transaction approval logs, key management policy documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement event logging for multisig Guardian actions, including proposal cancellations, with detailed signer attribution.
checklist_items.description: Provides an auditable trail for Guardian activities to prevent repudiation and establish accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or Governance System does not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure multisig contracts and governance modules to emit detailed events logging all signers for critical actions, and store these logs in immutable, accessible on-chain records.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Event logs emitted by the multisig contract showing individual signer addresses and timestamps.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational security protocols for Guardians, including secure key storage, regular key rotation, and incident response procedures.
checklist_items.description: Minimizes the risk of Guardian key compromise, preventing unauthorized proposal cancellations or malicious governance actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig implementation or operational procedures have vulnerabilities in key management and operational security.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Develop comprehensive operational security policies; utilize hardware security modules; conduct regular security audits; implement key rotation; enforce multi-factor authentication.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational security policy documentation, key management audit logs, incident response records.

checklist_items.title: Deploy smart contracts with formal verification and rigorous security audits, focusing on access controls and core logic.
checklist_items.description: Reduces the risk of privilege escalation or unauthorized administrative actions due to flawed smart contract code.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The deployed smart contract includes functions intended for administrative or privileged access, which may contain backdoors or flawed logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct formal verification of access control mechanisms; perform multiple independent security audits; include security review in the deployment process; design upgradability and administrative controls transparently.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, deployment logs, and source code reviews.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHA, and submission fees for RFRV proposals to prevent spamming and abuse of the whitelisting process.
checklist_items.description: Helps mitigate denial-of-service attacks against the RFRV submission platform from malicious actors flooding the system with fake submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective rate-limiting, anti-spam measures, or submission costs for the RFRV channels.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure submission portals to include CAPTCHA, rate-limiting, and possibly fee requirements; monitor submission patterns for spam detection.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of submission rates, CAPTCHA implementation records, fee collection records.

checklist_items.title: Educate users and implement security warnings to verify transaction details carefully before signing in wallets, especially during token swaps and reward claims.
checklist_items.description: Reduces the risk of tampering with transaction parameters on compromised or malicious frontend interfaces.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not meticulously verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: User education campaigns; include warnings and detailed transaction summaries in wallet interfaces; promote hardware wallets for critical transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials, UI/UX design standards, security awareness documentation.
checklist_items.owner: User Experience and Security Awareness Team

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication protocols (HTTPS, end-to-end encryption) for all off-chain proposal and transaction submissions.
checklist_items.description: Prevents tampering or interception of sensitive data during transit, protecting integrity and confidentiality.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV submission platform may not have sufficient integrity or confidentiality controls, making it vulnerable to interception or modification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all proposal submission channels use HTTPS; implement digital signatures; employ encryption and secure storage; regularly review and update communication security policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network security audit logs, SSL/TLS certificates, digital signature verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Apply rate-limiting, CAPTCHA, and submission fees to RFRV proposals to minimize spam and DoS attacks.
checklist_items.description: Helps ensure that the governance proposal platform maintains availability and integrity under attack.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV review process may be vulnerable to spam, and system capacity is limited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure proposal submission portals with rate limits, CAPTCHA, and optional fees; implement automated spam detection; monitor for suspicious activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Submission logs, CAPTCHA validation logs, fee transaction records.

checklist_items.title: Require hardware wallets and educate users on secure private key management to prevent private key disclosure.
checklist_items.description: Reduces the risk of private key disclosure, preventing impersonation, authorization repudiation, and asset theft.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User private keys/seed phrase (Asset 18) could be disclosed due to insecure storage, phishing, malware, or use of compromised Wallet Software.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallet usage; educate users on best practices for seed phrase security; recommend reputable wallet software; implement multi-factor security measures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User security policy documents, hardware wallet adoption rates, user education program documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain identity verification for proposal submissions
checklist_items.description: Require proposers to prove their identity via cryptographic signatures or verified wallet addresses before submitting Reward Vault whitelisting proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The review process by the Berachain Foundation (Component 8) may not verify the true identity of the submitter.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce cryptographic signature verification and maintain an on-chain registry of authorized submitters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract logs showing valid cryptographic signatures matching registered identities.

checklist_items.title: Require cryptographic signatures for all governance proposal submissions from privileged roles
checklist_items.description: Ensure all proposals submitted by the Berachain Foundation (Actor 8) are signed with their private key and verifiable by the governance contract, preventing spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system might not verify the authenticity of proposal submissions from the Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate digital signatures on all proposal submissions with on-chain verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs and proposal submission records.

checklist_items.title: Add nonce and timestamp to proposal submissions to prevent replay and tampering
checklist_items.description: Require proposal payloads to include a nonce and timestamp, which are signed and verified, making tampering with proposal data detectable and preventing replay attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might accept proposals without verifying data integrity.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require signatures over payloads with embedded nonce and timestamp; verify signatures and freshness on receipt.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal data with embedded nonce and timestamp, along with verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement role-based access control with multi-factor authentication for proposal submission processes
checklist_items.description: Require multiple, independent approvals (multi-sig) for proposals from privileged actors like the Foundation to prevent unauthorized or spoofed proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposals are submitted dishonestly by compromised or malicious actors.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multi-signature approval workflows with multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of multi-sig approvals matching authorized identities.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict on-chain validation for reward allocation updates
checklist_items.description: Smart contracts managing reward allocations should validate input parameters against defined constraints (e.g., total % not exceeding 100%), preventing malicious or erroneous changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may contain vulnerabilities allowing reward allocation constraints to be bypassed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement assertions and require multiple, verified inputs before accepting reward allocation changes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Contract code with formal verification records and audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-factor authorization for reward allocation changes
checklist_items.description: Any change to reward distributions should require multiple independent approvals or signatures to prevent single-point malicious modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validators could maliciously or accidentally change reward allocations if they have unchecked priviledges.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multi-signature approval schemes with separate devices or custodians for reward configuration.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Approval logs showing multi-party signatures for reward changes.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring and alerting for reward allocation anomalies
checklist_items.description: Continuously monitor reward allocation parameters and trigger alerts if allocations violate constraints or are inconsistent with past behavior, enabling rapid detection of tampering or misconfiguration.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Malicious or erroneous reward allocations may go unnoticed without monitoring.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy operational monitoring tools with anomaly detection on reward distribution parameters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs, alert records, and anomaly reports.

checklist_items.title: Secure wallet private keys used by validator operators
checklist_items.description: Validator operators must implement hardware security modules (HSMs) or secure enclave solutions to store private keys, preventing unauthorized access and spoofing of validator signatures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator keys are vulnerable to theft or misuse if stored insecurely.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware security modules or secure enclave technology for key storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key storage security configuration audits; hardware module accessibility logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish comprehensive validator key rotation and incident response procedures
checklist_items.description: Regularly rotate validator keys and develop procedures to revoke and replace keys promptly in case of compromise, reducing the risk of spoofed signatures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator keys may be compromised or spied upon.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Define key rotation schedules and incident response plans; conduct drills.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Rotation logs and incident response documentation.

checklist_items.title: Audit validator consensus key management and multisig controls
checklist_items.description: Regularly audit the key management and multisig control setup for validators, including access control policies and operational procedures, to prevent unauthorized key sharing or malicious access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak management or oversight of validator keys increases risk of spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement formal audits, role-based access controls, and multi-party control procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, key access logs, and control policies.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong access control in Reward Vault smart contracts to verify that only designated Token Managers can add or modify incentives.
checklist_items.description: To prevent spoofing threats, ensure Reward Vault contracts authenticate the caller as the legitimate Token Manager for that vault and incentive type.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review and audit logs demonstrating access control implementation.

checklist_items.title: Use secure and auditable wallet interfaces for Token Managers to set and modify incentive parameters, verifying all parameters before signing transactions.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate tampering threats, ensure Token Managers interact with smart contracts through secure, trusted wallets and verify transaction parameters carefully before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing verification steps and UI screenshots of parameter review.

checklist_items.title: Establish daily monitoring and alerts for Reward Vault capacity limits and transaction failures to prevent denial of service due to capacity exhaustion or contract bugs.
checklist_items.description: To address availability and DoS threats, implement monitoring of incentive type limits, transaction success rates, and contract error logs in Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults have a functional limit on the number of active incentive types.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Reward Vault smart contracts should be thoroughly audited for bugs and designed with clear, manageable limits, possibly including mechanisms for expiring or replacing old/unused incentives. Protocols should monitor vault capacity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboards, alert logs, and audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Enforce transaction parameter validation and limit setting in reward allocation functions to prevent reward manipulation or excess allocation.
checklist_items.description: To prevent elevation of privilege and tampering, implement on-chain validation of reward allocation percentages to ensure they do not exceed system-defined constraints.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are specific constraints on reward allocations (e.g., total must not exceed 100%).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure rigorous testing and auditing of the smart contracts managing reward allocations to verify that all constraints are correctly and robustly enforced. Implement on-chain checks that prevent invalid allocations from being committed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Contract deployment verification reports showing validation logic.

checklist_items.title: Configure multisig governance processes with multiple stakeholders and implement multisig key management best practices to prevent unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate spoofing and privilege escalation threats, ensure multisig proposals (like proposal cancellations) require multiple independent signatures and are secured against key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Compromise of Guardian keys can allow spoofed proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Robust multisig implementation, secure key management practices for Guardians, monitoring of cancellation events, and potentially a delay/challenge period for cancellations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction logs, key management policies, audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographically secure signature verification for all governance proposal submissions to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.description: To prevent spoofing from impersonation or data tampering, verify all proposals with cryptographic signatures from verified identity keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data can be altered by an attacker during transmission.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposal submissions to be signed by a verifiable cryptographic key, and verify signatures on-chain before processing proposals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Verification logs showing signature validation process and signed payloads.

checklist_items.title: Create and enforce a proposal submission from validated and known addresses using cryptographic signatures linked to the berachain foundation’s verified key set.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate impersonation threats, only accept proposals that are signed with cryptographically verified keys belonging to the berachain foundation’s official key sets.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal origin can be spoofed without proper signature verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multisignatures or signed messages from verified key sets, and verify signatures before processing proposals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal payloads and signature verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure communication protocols and operational security standards for Guardians to prevent off-chain proposal review leaks.
checklist_items.description: To address information disclosure risks, establish secure, confidentiality-preserving channels for Guardians to review and discuss proposals before submitting on-chain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians conduct off-chain discussions that could leak sensitive proposal info.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish secure communication protocols (e.g., encrypted messaging, access controls) for Guardians' off-chain discussions and review processes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational security training logs, secure communication setup documentation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce rate-limiting and use CAPTCHAs or transaction fees on proposal submission portals to prevent spam and submit overload.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate spam and DoS threats, enforce strict submission limits using CAPTCHAs, submission fees, and rate-limiting to control proposal load.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV/Miscover process lacks rate-limiting, enabling spam attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement CAPTCHA, submission fees, and rate-limiting controls for proposal submission portals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Server logs demonstrating rate limiting and CAPTCHA enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification for Reward Vault whitelisting proposals submitted via the Governance System
checklist_items.description: Verify that proposals for whitelisting Reward Vaults are signed by a verifiable on-chain identity of the Berachain Foundation to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) authenticates proposals based solely on off-chain submission without cryptographic verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed by a verified multisig wallet controlled by the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal payloads and validation logs showing signature verification.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access controls within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only designated Token Managers can add or modify incentives
checklist_items.description: Restrict functions that add or modify incentives in the Reward Vault to addresses verified as Token Managers through on-chain roles, preventing spoofed incentive offers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access control and conduct formal verification of access control logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code audits, role assignment records, and test case results verifying access restrictions.

checklist_items.title: Require explicit verification and validation of all incentive parameter transactions signed by the Token Manager
checklist_items.description: Ensure that any transaction to set incentive rates or deposit tokens is meticulously reviewed by the Token Manager's signer before submission, possibly via hardware wallet or secure interface.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious interface to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage direct interaction with verified, secure wallets and educate Token Managers on diligent transaction verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction review logs showing detailed verification steps and confirmation.

checklist_items.title: Implement gas and token sufficiency checks before submitting incentive management transactions
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Token Manager's wallet has sufficient gas and deposit tokens to complete incentive updates, and monitor Reward Vault capacity limits to prevent DoS conditions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults have maximum capacity and the Token Manager may run out of gas or tokens.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design contracts with capacity limits, and include pre-transaction checks or user prompts for resource sufficiency.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction simulation logs showing resource availability checks.

checklist_items.title: Establish multi-signature controls and operational procedures for setting incentive rates to prevent excessive or malicious adjustments
checklist_items.description: Require multiple independent approvals before changing incentive rates, including rate bounds and change logs, to prevent unauthorized or malicious tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Protocols lack multi-sig or governance oversight on incentive rate changes.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-signature approval process and enforce pre-defined rate bounds.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multi-sig confirmation logs and change request documentation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce maximum and sane incentive rate bounds within the smart contract logic
checklist_items.description: Design the incentive rate setting function to include hard-coded or parameterizable upper limits, with validation before acceptance, to prevent unrealistic or harmful rates.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The smart contract does not currently enforce limits on incentive rates.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add validation in setter functions to enforce predefined maximums.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code with validation logic and tests.

checklist_items.title: Require that the proposal data submitted by the Foundation be cryptographically signed and verified on-chain
checklist_items.description: The Foundation should submit proposals with digital signatures validated within the governance contract to prevent spoofed or tampered submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance proposal process currently relies solely on off-chain submission without cryptographic verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require on-chain signed proposal payloads validated against on-chain authority keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Verification logs demonstrating signature validation against on-chain keys.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure, encrypted channels for off-chain proposal submissions
checklist_items.description: Use HTTPS, TLS, or other encryption methods for off-chain proposal data transmission to prevent tampering and eavesdropping during transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data during transit may be susceptible to tampering or interception.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use TLS or end-to-end encryption for all proposal submission channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network logs showing encryption protocols and verification.

checklist_items.title: Establish strict criteria and identity verification for Foundation proposal submission access
checklist_items.description: Require multi-factor authentication, digital signatures, or other strong mechanisms for the Foundation (Actor 8) when submitting proposals to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Foundation's proposal submission system may be vulnerable to impersonation if not secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce multi-factor authentication and cryptographic signing for proposal submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing MFA or signature validation processes.

checklist_items.title: Monitor and log multisig Guardian actions with detailed signer attribution
checklist_items.description: Ensure on-chain events for Guardian multisig operations include detailed signer information to facilitate accountability and non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The current on-chain logging does not fully attribute multisig actions to individual signers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement detailed event logging for multisig operations with signer attribution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain event logs verifying signers for proposal cancellations.

checklist_items.title: Require direct or signed on-chain submission for proposal cancellations to prevent off-chain tampering
checklist_items.description: Design the cancellation process so that Guardian multisig actions can only be executed via explicit on-chain signatures or direct on-chain transactions, minimizing tampering risks during off-chain relays.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Cancellation signals may be relayed off-chain without cryptographic guarantees.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require that multisig guardians submit signatures on-chain for proposal cancellations, avoiding off-chain message interception or modification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain signatures and transaction records confirming proper execution.

checklist_items.title: Implement guardian operational security, key management, and multisig best practices
checklist_items.description: Ensure guardians use hardware security modules, secure key storage, and operational protocols to prevent key compromise and unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Compromise of Guardian keys can lead to illegitimate proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules, regular key rotation, and multi-party key management for guardians.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management procedure documentation and audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify that Reward Vault contracts enforce strict access control for incentive management functions
checklist_items.description: Ensure only the designated Token Manager addresses can execute add, offer, or manage incentive functions within Reward Vault contracts, as per mitigation strategies addressing Spoofing threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address can execute related functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or contract code demonstrating strict access control modifiers on incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain provenance verification for governance proposals submitted by the Berachain Foundation
checklist_items.description: Require proposal submissions to include a cryptographic signature or on-chain attestation verifying they originate from legitimate, verified Foundation addresses, addressing spoofing threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation (Actor 8).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposal signatures from a verified Foundation multisig address and verify those signatures on-chain before accepting proposals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal payload with validation logs confirming signature verification.

checklist_items.title: Set explicit gas limits and resource checks in Reward Vault management transactions
checklist_items.description: Require that all incentive management transactions specify sufficient gas and resource limits to prevent failure or denial of service due to resource exhaustion or contract bugs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults have a functional limit on the number of distinct active incentive types.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement transaction pre-conditions to verify sufficient gas and resources before execution; monitor gas usage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing resource checks and gas limits.

checklist_items.title: Implement signature verification for proposal actions from the Foundation and Guardians
checklist_items.description: Require on-chain verification of signatures on proposals and cancellation signals from the Foundation and Guardians, ensuring only authorized entities can perform these actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not verify the authenticity of the cancellation signal if a sufficient number of Guardian keys are compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require multi-party signature verification and incorporate on-chain signature validation for all governance proposals and cancellation signals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs and event records showing on-chain validation.

checklist_items.title: Validate transaction details in the wallet prior to signing
checklist_items.description: Require users to verify all transaction parameters (amounts, addresses, slippage, etc.) in their wallet interface before signing, to prevent tampering via compromised frontends.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: dApp frontends can be compromised (e.g., DNS hijacking, malicious code injection).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement wallet prompts that display transaction details for user verification and encourage careful review.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots or logs showing user verification steps.

checklist_items.title: Ensure secure private key management and wallet security best practices
checklist_items.description: Promote the use of hardware wallets and secure seed phrase backups, and educate users on safe private key handling to mitigate private key disclosure risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may store seed phrases insecurely or be susceptible to malware and phishing.
