checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage hardware wallet use, secure backup procedures, and avoid malicious or compromised wallet software.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User policies, user reports, or wallet audit results.

checklist_items.title: Verify that smart contract code is audited and includes explicit access control mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Ensure that deployed smart contracts governing incentives, vaults, and governance are audited and contain clear, enforceable access restrictions, reducing risk of elevation of privilege or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Developer contracts may contain malicious code or flawed access control logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require formal audits and code reviews focusing on access control and include automated verification tools.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, code review summaries, and formal verification results.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication and robust key management for governance and Guardian accounts
checklist_items.description: Require multi-factor authentication for access to multisig wallets and account management, and enforce strong key management policies to prevent unauthorized control over governance operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Multisig management may be vulnerable if private keys are compromised or poorly secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt multi-party signatures with hardware security modules and operational security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, key rotation logs, and access audit trails.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only designated Token Managers can add or manage incentives.
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based permissions or address whitelisting in the smart contract to prevent unauthorized incentive modifications. This mitigates spoofing threats related to incentive management.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating restricted access to incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require all Token Manager transactions involving incentive parameter changes to be verified through trusted, secure interfaces, and verify transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.description: Educate Token Managers to use secure wallets and verify all transaction parameters meticulously, especially for setting incentive rates or depositing tokens. This process control reduces tampering risks during incentive updates.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of transaction verification procedures; user confirmations.

checklist_items.title: Implement and enforce transaction confirmation procedures requiring users to verify all critical parameters in their wallets before signing, particularly for reward configuration or territorial actions.
checklist_items.description: Require users to review transaction details in secure wallets before signing; incorporate transaction previews and checksums in the wallet UI to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator's private key for configuring reward allocations is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong wallet and UI practices for transaction review; consider using hardware wallets and multi-party confirmation for critical updates.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User confirmation logs or screenshots of transaction review screens.

checklist_items.title: Establish on-chain, multi-signature or governance-enforced controls for setting incentive parameters, including maximum allowed rates and deposit amounts.
checklist_items.description: Use governance mechanisms or multi-sig wallets to approve incentive parameter changes, preventing single-point compromises from leading to malicious configurations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious interface to alter incentive parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement governance-approved, multi-signature controls for incentive configurations; restrict who can modify incentive parameters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit trail of governance approvals or multi-signature signatures for incentive changes.

checklist_items.title: Monitor validator activity for anomalous reward allocation and reward emission patterns to detect potential manipulation or abuse.
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain or off-chain monitoring tools to flag unusual reward setting transactions or reward distribution irregularities, enabling timely detection of reward manipulation attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in how validator reward allocations could be manipulated.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use monitoring tools to track reward allocation transactions and parameters; establish alerts for suspicious activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring reports, logs of reward setting transactions.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic and operational safeguards to prevent private key compromise of validator nodes, including secure key management and regular key rotations.
checklist_items.description: Use hardware security modules (HSMs), multisig authorization, and strict operational security procedures to mitigate risks of validator key compromise, reducing spoofing and elevation of privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish secure key management practices (HSMs, multisignatures); implement key rotation policies and monitoring.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management logs, key rotation audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Monitor validator activeness and participation metrics to detect absenteeism or collusion that could lead to network DoS or consensus failures.
checklist_items.description: Deploy network health monitoring tools to track validator uptime, signing activity, and consensus participation. Establish thresholds and alerts for inactivity or anomalous behavior.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Failure of validators to participate in consensus can cause network halts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement validators' liveness checks, slashing for inactivity, and diversified validator set policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validator activity logs, network performance reports.

checklist_items.title: Apply network and consensus security best practices, including cryptographic signature validations, slashing mechanisms, and regular audits of validator keys and behaviors.
checklist_items.description: Ensure correctness of consensus logic, validate signatures correctly, and enforce penalties on suspicious or malicious validator behavior, in line with protocol security standards.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Compromise of a validator's private keys could allow spoofing in consensus messages.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement signature validation, slash malicious nodes, and conduct regular audits of validator operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Consensus validation logs, slashing event records.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access controls on the Reward Vault smart contract functions managing incentives
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only designated Token Managers can add, offer, or modify incentives within the Reward Vault, authenticated via on-chain address verification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing only authorized Token Manager addresses execute incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require transaction parameter verification in user interfaces before signing incentive management transactions
checklist_items.description: Advise Token Managers to use secure, audited interfaces to verify and confirm all transaction parameters, such as incentive rate and token amounts, before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Confirmation logs from the wallet showing user verified transaction details prior to signing.

checklist_items.title: Configure multimodal authentication for proposal submissions from privileged actors
checklist_items.description: Implement multi-factor authentication and cryptographic signing for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ensuring submissions can be cryptographically verified and linked to the authorized entity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions from the Foundation, e.g., multisig signatures, on-chain verification, and cryptographic proof.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain evidence of proposal signatures and cryptographic verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Ensure multisig safeguard with secure key management and rotation protocols for Guardian keys
checklist_items.description: Enforce rigorous key management practices including hardware security modules, periodic key rotation, and multi-factor authentication for Guardian multisig keys utilized in proposal cancellation functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig implementation, which authorizes proposal cancellations, might have vulnerabilities in member management or signature validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits, secure key management protocols, key rotation policies, and multi-factor key authorization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access, rotation records, and multisig signature verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain event logging for multisignature guardian actions
checklist_items.description: Configure the multisig contract and governance system to emit detailed, immutable events logging individual guardian signers' participation in proposal cancellations and other critical actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or Governance System does not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure the multisig and Governance System emit detailed events logging all individual signers for critical actions like proposal cancellation. Maintain immutable and accessible on-chain records.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Event logs accessible on-chain with signer details for each multisig action.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to authenticate the Token Manager for incentive management functions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure only the designated Token Manager address can execute functions to add, offer, or manage incentives in the Reward Vault, preventing impersonation or unauthorized access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring only the designated Token Manager address can execute incentive actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or transaction records showing only authorized Token Manager addresses performing incentive functions.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-factor authentication for proposals submission from the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.description: Strengthen the proposal submission process by requiring multi-factor authentication or cryptographic signatures to verify the identity of the Foundation, reducing spoofing risk.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify the authenticity of proposals from the Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for Foundation proposal submissions, such as signed messages or multisig approval.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit trail of proposal submissions with cryptographic signatures verifying Foundation's identity.

checklist_items.title: Sign all reward vault whitelisting proposals with a cryptographically verifiable signature from the Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.description: Require the Foundation's proposal data to be signed with a private key corresponding to a verified on-chain identity, enabling the governance system to verify the proposal's authenticity and integrity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not verify the integrity of all proposal data against signed payloads.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require all proposals from the Foundation to include a signature over the complete proposal data, verified on-chain before acceptance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Successful verification of cryptographic signatures and audit logs matching proposal data to Foundation identity.

checklist_items.title: Add rate-limiting and CAPTCHA to the RFRV submission platform.
checklist_items.description: Implement controls like CAPTCHA and rate limiting on RFRV submission interfaces to prevent spam, spam flooding, or denial-of-service attacks against the proposal review process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV submission process may not have robust mechanisms to verify the legitimacy or prevent spam submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement CAPTCHA and rate limiting on RFRV submission channels, and monitor submission volume.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System logs showing CAPTCHA enforcement and rate limiting during submission periods.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all Smart Contracts involved in Governor proposals and Incentive mechanisms are thoroughly audited and formally verified.
checklist_items.description: Perform comprehensive security audits and formal verification of smart contracts managing proposals, incentives, and reward distributions to prevent tampering and logic flaws.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contract code, despite audits, may contain hidden vulnerabilities or logic flaws.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multiple independent audits, formal verification, and bug bounty programs for all relevant smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proofs, and bug bounty findings confirming the absence of critical vulnerabilities.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure key management, multi-signature controls, and operational security practices for Guardian multisigs.
checklist_items.description: Enforce strict key management policies, multi-factor authentication, and regular key rotation for Guardian multisig wallets to prevent unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig implementation, which authorizes proposal cancellations, might have vulnerabilities or compromised keys.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish secure offline key storage, multi-factor authentication, regular key rotation, and real-time monitoring of Guardian multisig transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, key management policies, and incident reports demonstrating secure practices.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only the designated Token Manager address can execute incentive management functions.
checklist_items.description: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding or managing incentives must restrict these functions to authorized Token Managers to prevent spoofing threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring only authorized Token Managers can execute management functions. Token Managers must maintain secure private keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing access control restrictions and authorization checks in the smart contract.
checklist_items.owner: Security Team or Smart Contract Developers

checklist_items.title: Require all wallet interactions with the Reward Vault to verify transaction parameters explicitly before signing.
checklist_items.description: Token Managers and users must meticulously verify all incentive-related transaction parameters (e.g., rate, token address, deposit amount) in their wallet interfaces before signing transactions, to prevent tampering via compromised frontends.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Users must use secure and trusted wallet interfaces that display all transaction details clearly, and verify all parameters before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User confirmation logs showing transaction parameter verification in the wallet.
checklist_items.owner: User Education and Wallet Security Team

checklist_items.title: Ensure the Reward Vault smart contract has implemented safeguards and limits against setting excessively high incentive rates, including hard-coded maximums and safety checks.
checklist_items.description: To prevent economic sabotage via setting impossible or damaging incentive rates, the Reward Vault should include explicit constraints on incentive parameters, enforced within the contract logic.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective hard-coded upper bounds or sanity checks for the incentive rate in the smart contract.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Incorporate in the Reward Vault smart contract explicit, enforced maximum limits and validation checks on all incentive parameters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification records confirming enforcement of incentive parameter constraints.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain signature verification for all Reward Vault proposals submitted by the Berachain Foundation to prevent impersonation or spoofed proposals.
checklist_items.description: To establish non-repudiation and prevent spoofing of Reward Vault whitelisting proposals, all submissions from the Foundation should be signed with a cryptographically verifiable key, with signatures verified on-chain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) relies on authentic proposal submissions; signature verification is not currently enforced.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require all proposal submissions to be accompanied by a digital signature from a secure, private key held by the Foundation, with on-chain verification of the signature before processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction logs showing signature verification processes and signatures attached to proposals.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure, authenticated off-chain communication channels for Guardians to submit cancellation signals, with cryptographic protections against tampering.
checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering with cancellation signals during off-chain relay, Guardians should use secure, signed messaging channels, and the system should verify signatures on-chain before acting.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The cancellation signal may transit insecure off-chain channels, allowing tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require Guardians to sign cancellation messages with their private keys, and verify signatures on-chain before executing cancel actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of signed messages and on-chain verification results.

checklist_items.title: Enhance multi-signature key management for Guardian keys, including secure hardware modules, operational procedures, and regular key rotation to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.description: Guardian multisig keys should be stored and managed using hardware security modules (HSMs), with operational controls, multi-factor access, and regular rotation to reduce the risk of key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardian private keys are stored in potentially vulnerable environments; operational procedures may lack security best practices.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware key management, enforce strict access controls, multi-factor authentication, and regular key rotation policies for Guardian keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational security audits, key management logs, and HSM configuration reviews.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain enforcement of reward allocation constraints, including total percentage caps and per-vault limits, with formal verification and code auditing.
checklist_items.description: The smart contracts managing reward allocations should include explicit checks that enforce maximum total percentages and individual vault caps, confirmed via formal verification and tested in audits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contract logic for reward allocation may contain vulnerabilities allowing constraint bypass.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use formal verification and extensive testing to verify enforcement of reward allocation constraints within the smart contract code.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Formal verification reports, audit reports documenting constraint enforcement, and test results.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control in Reward Vault smart contracts to ensure only designated Token Managers can add or modify incentives.
checklist_items.description: To prevent spoofing attacks where unauthorized entities add malicious incentives, access control must verify caller identities accurately.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing restricted access to incentive management functions.

checklist_items.title: Require all transaction parameters for incentive rate updates to be verified meticulously by the Token Manager before signing.
checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering with incentives, Token Managers should confirm all details in their wallets prior to transaction signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager might use a compromised or malicious dApp frontend to interact with the Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Token Managers must use secure and audited interfaces for interacting with smart contracts, or interact directly via trusted wallet software. Always meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing. dApp frontends should implement security best practices (e.g., CSP, SRI).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Record of Token Manager verifying transaction details before signing.

checklist_items.title: Implement appropriate rate limits and validation checks to prevent Validator reward configuration skewing or excessive changes.
checklist_items.description: To avoid risks of reward misconfiguration, enforce limits on how often and how much validators can alter reward settings.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Reward Vault can be whitelisted but later become non-functional or problematic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce gas costs for reward allocation changes. Implement and enforce allocation constraints (e.g., max percentage per vault). Utilize default allocations if a validator's custom allocation is problematic. Monitor Reward Vault health and functionality.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing reward configuration changes and enforcement of limits.

checklist_items.title: Set transaction value upper bounds and include sanity checks when setting incentive rates.
checklist_items.description: Establish and enforce maximum reasonable incentive rates in smart contracts to prevent overly high settings that could deplete reserves rapidly.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no effective hard-coded upper bounds or sanity checks in the smart contract for the incentive rate relative to the total amount of tokens deposited for that incentive.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Protocols should establish clear internal guidelines, review processes, and potentially multi-sig controls for setting/changing incentive rates. Consider implementing optional warnings or soft limits in any UI used by Token Managers. The existing rule that rates cannot be decreased while active (doc_21) is a partial mitigation against some forms of manipulation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing rate settings within bounds.

checklist_items.title: Require multisignature approval for governance proposals related to Reward Vault whitelisting.
checklist_items.description: To prevent unauthorized or malicious proposal submission, enforce multi-party approval mechanisms for governance actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation (Actor 8).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures. Regularly audit access and authorization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Evidence of multisig approval logs for proposal submissions.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all on-chain proposal submissions.
checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering with proposal data, enforce signature verification of proposal payloads to confirm integrity and origin.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The whitelisting proposal data submitted by the Berachain Foundation (Actor 8) could be altered by an attacker before being finalized by the Governance System.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require the proposer to sign the complete, final proposal payload with their private key. The Governance System must verify this signature against all parameters of the received data before processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs matching payloads.

checklist_items.title: Require proposals to be submitted directly on-chain from a verified, well-secured address.
checklist_items.description: To strengthen non-repudiation and prevent impersonation, enforce that proposals originate from known, controlled on-chain addresses linked to the trusted entity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The on-chain transaction record (e.g., 'from' address of the proposal submission) might be claimed by the Foundation as having been compromised at the time of submission to deny responsibility.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish that all proposals are submitted from a publicly declared, multi-sig controlled address with strict operational security. Maintain transparent records of proposal submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction logs linking proposals to verified addresses.

checklist_items.title: Employ secure communication channels and data integrity checks for off-chain proposal-related data exchanges.
checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering with proposal data during off-chain submission or review processes, ensure secure channels and implement checksums or signatures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The whitelisting proposal data submitted by the Foundation could be altered in transit, leading to malicious or incorrect proposal data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use HTTPS with TLS, implement digital signatures on proposal data, and use checksums to verify data integrity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed data hashes and audit logs of data transfer.

checklist_items.title: Require the Foundation to sign proposal payloads cryptographically when submitting whitelisting requests.
checklist_items.description: To address repudiation and strengthen accountability, mandate signed proposals from the Foundation with verifiable cryptographic signatures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The on-chain transaction record might be claimed by the Foundation as compromised or in doubt; signatures provide proof of origin.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require all submissions to include a cryptographic signature from the Foundation's verified key, and verify signatures during proposal processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain logs verifying signatures against submitted proposal data.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust access control in Reward Vault contracts to ensure only designated Token Managers can add or modify incentives.
checklist_items.description: Based on the mitigation that access control should be strict within the Reward Vault smart contract, verify that functions managing incentives are protected by appropriate access restrictions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or contract source code verifying access modifiers for incentive management functions.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure that access restrictions are properly implemented and tested in the Reward Vault contracts.

checklist_items.title: Require secure user interface practices in BeraHub for delegation and incentive claiming functions.
checklist_items.description: Check if BeraHub employs HTTPS, content security policies (CSP), and code integrity measures to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BeraHub frontend could be compromised or malicious, leading to transaction tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong frontend security measures (CSP, SRI), enforce HTTPS, and conduct regular security assessments.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files, security audit reports, or security assessment documentation.
checklist_items.notes: Verify that the interface implements end-to-end encryption and content security policies.

checklist_items.title: Audit the multisig governance process for proposal cancellation to ensure event logging and signer attribution.
checklist_items.description: Check that multisig transaction logs include detailed signer signatures and that events are emitted with signer identities to prevent repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig contract or governance system does not log or make easily queryable the individual signers for each cancellation action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enhance event emitting in multisig contracts to include signer addresses and timestamps, and store these logs securely on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs showing signer signatures and timestamps for recent cancellations.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure multisig signatures are properly logged and accessible for accountability.

checklist_items.title: Require signed and verified proposal data during whitelisting submission.
checklist_items.description: Implement digital signature verification for RFRV submissions to prevent tampering or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal data is vulnerable to modification after being sent by the Berachain Foundation but before being immutably recorded.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signatures on all proposal data, verify signatures before acceptance, and reject unsigned or mismatched data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Cryptographic signatures matching the purported sender and proposal contents.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure proposal submissions are cryptographically signed by the submitting entity.

checklist_items.title: Verify private key management practices to prevent collapse of confidentiality.
checklist_items.description: Check for usage of hardware wallets, hardware security modules (HSMs), and secure key storage practices.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys/seed phrase (Asset 18), if disclosed, grant full control over the user's account and assets.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets for private key storage, implement multi-factor authentication, and promote secure backup practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet configuration audit logs, key storage policies, and user security guidelines.
checklist_items.notes: Assess wallet setup procedures and user education.

checklist_items.title: Implement formal verification for critical smart contracts managing incentives and rewards.
checklist_items.description: Apply formal methods to verify correctness of contract logic related to incentives, boost, and reward distribution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in how validator stake, $BGT boost, or other PoL parameters are calculated or applied could allow a validator to illegitimately increase their influence or rewards.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use formal verification tools and techniques on incentive, boost, and reward distribution smart contracts before deployment.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Formal verification reports, proof of verification, and review records.
checklist_items.notes: Use formal verification tools such as VerX, Certora, or KEVM.

checklist_items.title: Monitor and enforce reward allocation constraints within PoL mechanism.
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks and validation to ensure validator reward allocations adhere to system constraints (e.g., max percentage per vault).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in the PoL mechanism's reward allocation logic could allow a validator to illegitimately increase their influence or rewards.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce on-chain rules and constraints on rebalance operations, including maximum allocation percentages.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction verification logs illustrating reward allocations are within constraints.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure on-chain validation enforces total allocation percentage equals 100%.

checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users rely on the integrity of BeraHub (Component 25) for correct incentive claims.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong frontend security measures, educate users, and verify transaction details.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports of BeraHub frontend code, user confirmation logs, SSL/TLS certificate validation.

checklist_items.title: Deploy multi-sig governance processes for proposal submissions and include delay mechanisms to prevent immediate execution of malicious proposals.
checklist_items.description: Based on mitigation strategies for high-impact governance actions to enforce multi-party approvals and timelocks, verify that a governance process requires multiple signatures and has appropriate delay periods.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System (Component 29) might not adequately verify that a whitelisting proposal originates from the legitimate Berachain Foundation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions originating from the Berachain Foundation, ideally using a secure multisig wallet with stringent operational procedures. Regularly audit access and authorization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction logs showing multiple signers, audit reports of governance process.

checklist_items.title: Implement security measures such as secure key storage, key rotation, and operational security best practices for validator operational keys.
checklist_items.description: Based on mitigation strategies for validator key compromise, ensure that validator keys are stored in hardware security modules, rotated regularly, and that operational procedures prevent unauthorized access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's private key used for signing blocks and consensus messages is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management practices including hardware security modules, key rotation, and operational security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit reports, key access logs, hardware security module configurations.

checklist_items.title: Configure and enforce on-chain reward allocation constraints with formal verification to prevent validators from setting invalid distributions.
checklist_items.description: Based on the mitigation that reward allocations should be validated against constraints, verify that smart contracts governing reward distribution implement these constraints correctly and are formally verified.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are specific constraints on reward allocations (e.g., total must not exceed 100%, individual vaults may have caps like 30%).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure rigorous testing and auditing of the smart contracts managing reward allocations to verify that all constraints are correctly and robustly enforced. Implement on-chain checks that prevent invalid allocations from being committed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Formal verification reports, smart contract audit reports verifying constraint enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to restrict incentive management functions to designated Token Managers.
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access control to ensure only authorized Token Managers can add or modify incentives in the Reward Vault, preventing impersonation or unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's current access control may be improperly implemented, allowing impersonation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce role-based access control in smart contracts, ensuring only designated Token Managers can manage incentives.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code audit reports demonstrating role restrictions for incentive functions.

checklist_items.title: Validate all incentive parameter transactions at the wallet interface before signing to prevent tampering by malicious frontend or compromised wallet software.
checklist_items.description: Require the Token Manager to verify incentive parameters such as rate, token address, and deposit amount within their trusted wallet before signing the transaction, mitigating tampering risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Token Manager may use a compromised interface or may not verify transaction details diligently.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure and trusted wallet interfaces that display and verify all transaction parameters before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet logs showing verification steps before transaction signing.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Reward Vault's capacity limits are monitored and contracts are audited to prevent denial of service due to capacity exhaustion.
checklist_items.description: Implement monitoring and alerting for Reward Vaults reaching their maximum incentive type capacity; audit smart contracts to handle edge cases gracefully and include mechanisms for expiring or replacing incentives.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults have predefined maximum incentive types; potential for bugs or resource exhaustion exists.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regularly audit Reward Vault contracts and set up monitoring for capacity limits with automated alerts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboards and audit reports confirming capacity handling.
checklist_items.owner: Operations and Smart Contract Audit Team

checklist_items.title: Implement multisig governance for setting high-risk parameters such as incentive rates, with review and soft limits to prevent economically destructive configurations.
checklist_items.description: Establish a multisignature process requiring multiple trusted parties to approve incentive rate changes; incorporate soft limits and warnings in UI to prevent accidental misconfigurations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The approach relies on governance procedures and not solely on smart contract safeguards.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce multi-party approval for critical parameter changes and implement UI safeguards for incentive management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Governance approval records and UI review logs.

checklist_items.title: Configure secure key management practices for Guardian multisig keys with regular rotation, hardware security modules (HSMs), and operational security protocols.
checklist_items.description: Use hardware security modules and strict operational procedures for Guardian keys; rotate keys periodically and monitor multisig signing activities for anomalies, reducing the risk of unauthorized multisig actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig's security depends on proper key management and operational best practices.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement HSMs, multi-factor authentication, key rotation, and activity monitoring for Guardian keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit logs, HSM configuration assessments.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain event logging for all Guardian multisig actions, including proposal cancellations, with details of individual signers.
checklist_items.description: Ensure multisig contracts emit detailed, immutable events capturing each signer's involvement during proposal cancellation, enabling accountability and dispute resolution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Lack of detailed logs impairs ability to attribute Guardian actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enhance multisig contracts to emit per-signer signatures and detailed cancellation events.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain event logs showing signer details for proposal cancellations.

checklist_items.title: Require proposals to be signed with cryptographic signatures that directly link to the Berachain Foundation's verified addresses, and verify signatures upon submission.
checklist_items.description: Implement signature verification for proposals originating from the Foundation's known addresses to prevent spoofing and impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal submissions might be spoofed if signatures are not verified.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signatures from verified Foundation addresses during proposal submission.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs and audit trail of proposal submissions.

checklist_items.title: Implement end-to-end data integrity mechanisms, such as signing proposal data payloads before submission, to prevent tampering during transit.
checklist_items.description: Require the Foundation to sign the complete proposal payload before submission, and verify signatures on receipt to ensure data integrity and authenticity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal data might be altered in transit.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use digital signatures for proposal data during submission, with verification on the Governance System side.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal payloads and verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Require the Berachain Foundation to submit proposals through a trusted, multi-factor authenticated platform with access controls and logs.
checklist_items.description: Ensure proposals are submitted via a platform employing multi-factor authentication, access controls, and detailed audit logs, to prevent impersonation and repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak submission process can allow impersonation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use a secure proposal submission platform with multi-factor authentication and comprehensive logging.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Submission logs and platform access controls documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure communication protocols and channel integrity protections for off-chain proposal reviews and cancellation signals.
checklist_items.description: Use secure, encrypted, and signed communication channels for off-chain Guardian communications related to proposal cancellations, to prevent tampering or interception.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Off-chain communication channels are vulnerable to tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use TLS, digital signatures, and secure messaging protocols for Guardian off-chain communications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication security audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement and enforce strong access control within the Reward Vault smart contract to ensure only the designated Token Manager address can add or manage incentives.
checklist_items.description: To prevent spoofing threats where unauthorized entities spoof the Token Manager identity, access control must restrict incentive management functions to authorized addresses.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for adding/managing incentives might be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strict access control within the Reward Vault smart contract, ensuring that only the designated Token Manager address for a specific vault and incentive type can execute functions to add, offer, or manage those incentives. Token Managers must practice secure private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review documents showing access control modifiers for incentive functions.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure the smart contract code explicitly restricts add/manage functions to only the authorized Token Manager address.
